issue_title: Pub / Sub Subscriber: CPU Usage eventually spikes to 100%
issue_labels: [{'id': 139685153, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20pubsub', 'name': 'api: pubsub', 'color': 'f8f8f8', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604244, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p1', 'name': 'priority: p1', 'color': 'ffa03e', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438580, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20bug', 'name': 'type: bug', 'color': 'd93f0b', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
This is a distillation of this report from **many** other issues. Large thanks to @anorth2 and @dmontag for reporting and helping refine the issue.

Issues that have been partially resolved (except for the CPU spike) are being collapsed into this one:

- #4274 
- #4563 
- #3965
- (more to come?)

----

**Core issue**: there is a [spinlock bug][1] in gRPC (present in all recent versions `1.6.x`, `1.7.x`, `1.8.1`). A "bandaid" [fix][2] exists but has not been merged (as of noon Pacific on December 15, 2017).

**Potential workaround**: compile `grpcio` from source ([e.g.][3]) while including the bandaid [fix][2] or just use the 64-bit `manylinux` [wheel][4] that I already created (I may be open to creating Mac OS X wheels, not sure about Windows)

[1]: https://github.com/grpc/grpc/issues/9688
[2]: https://github.com/grpc/grpc/pull/13665
[3]: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/issues/4274#issuecomment-351863030

issue_title: Pub/Sub API lists the wrong (slightly incompatible) type for Message.attributes
issue_body:
When a subscriber receives a message, the API says it has an 'attributes' member that is a dict:

https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/11e310ab9f63f06252ff2b9ada201fd8c11ce06c/pubsub/google/cloud/pubsub_v1/subscriber/message.py#L36

However, it is not, in fact, a dict; it is a protobuf map. The behaviour is quite different, which the Python protobuf API documentation explains at some length:

https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/reference/python-generated#map-fields

This is not a small difference... the semantic gap is substantial, because using the `.attributes` type in place of a dict breaks code like this:

```
message_rev = None
if some_condition:
  try:
    message_rev = int(message.attributes['revision'])
  except KeyError:
    message_rev = DEFAULT
```

In this case, instead of assigning the value `DEFAULT`, this code simply raises a new exception that doesn't happen with dicts (it's a ValueError, because the try-block will execute `int('')`). This is an incompatible, breaking change.

Again, this is a subtle, but important, API difference. At a minimum, it seems like the PubSub API docs should point to the Protobuf docs, instead of simply listing the incorrect type.

issue_title: PythonInterpreter fails to discover OSX Python binaries
issue_body:
The real path of OSX python binaries can look like so:
`/System/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/Resources/Python.app/Contents/MacOS/Python`

The binary discovery logic in PythonInterpreter misses binaries like these due to case-sensitive regexes here: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/blob/master/pex/interpreter.py#L181

This was discovered in https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/issues/1908

issue_title: __init__.py in test modules causing import error
issue_body:
So if I have a test filesystem structure like:
```
- src/python/project/module
  - __init__.py
  - xxx.py
  - yyy.py
- tests/python/project/module
  - __init__.py
  - test_xxx.py
  - test_yyy.py
```
And inside `test_xxx.py` I import some thing from `xxx.py`.  I'll end up getting error import from `xxx.py`.

But if I remove the `__init__.py` from `test/python/project/module` folder, everything works.  I checked `twitter/commons` project and seems like it's not using `__init__.py` in all the test modules.

What's the reason for this behavior and could we at least document this behavior in the official Pants docs?  It took me hours to figure out this behavior.  Thanks.

in order to discover unknown bugs in areas of pants that are untested by our internal CI sandboxing, we'll also want to run all of the pants test with this flag enabled.

there are a few approaches to this:

1) expand the usage of the `@ensure_engine` decorator (whitelist approach)
2) enable the behavior of `@ensure_engine` by default and invert the whitelisting approach in #1 to except specific tests (blacklist approach)
3) enable a completely separate jenkins job/configuration to mirror CI with `--enable-v2-engine` as default configuration

additionally, initial smoke testing via PR may prove useful in uncovering an initial survey of the state of things - as well as informing the initial round of new bugs shaken out from this.

issue_title: Introduce a way to exclude particular BUILD files from being processed by pants
issue_body:
Why we need this: while using `npm install` different BUILD.md files created, and they are not valid pants BUILD files. With this files in repo some pants commands fail to run such as `./pants compile ::` and `./pants dependees`.

Currently pants have two mechanisms to deal with it: `--spec-excludes` and `--exclude-target-regexp`. 
`--exclude-target-regexp` states that it can be useful in case of broken BUILD files:
> Useful with ::, to ignore broken BUILD files.

It's sounds little bit inconsistent as the name of option suggests that it's deal with targets, not with build files. Also current implementation doing this filtering only in CmdLineSpecParser, that's why, for example, `./pants dependees` will be still broken in case of broken BUILD file in repo.
So I think `--exclude-target-regexp` should care only about targets.

Currently I see two main approaches to exclude BUILD files: introduce option like `--excluded-build-extensions` or make `--spec-excludes` support globs.
I prefer second approach more because it's cover all usecases the first one fixes and doesn't look more complex in terms of implementation (except there are should not be any performance downgrades), but it's not create one more option.

For the first approach we need to propagate excluded extensions to all places which using `_is_buildfile_name` method, which comes immediately to `BuildFile#_get_all_build_files` and `BuildFile#scan_buildfiles`. But for the second usage it's already with spec_excludes, and for the first it's should contain excludes any way (both in `--excluded-build-extensions` and `--spec-excludes`) so I can't see significant difference here. What is good about current `--spec-excludes` implementation I think that actually spec_excludes used only in `BuildFile#scan_buildfiles` (but I'm not 100% sure).

About performance downgrades: with new `--spec-excludes` semantic pants will not try to search targets declaration in any directory or file which matches with any pattern in `--spec-excludes`, that's why it will be almost identical in terms of performance to current solution.

issue_title: ./pants idea <target> is adding compilation tasks as dependencies
issue_body:
When executing 'goal idea', pants is doing too much work. It is invoking the compile and resources steps.


I encountered this issue when compiling from pants built from commit:

```
commit 193e6c4d57d3bee2a99334b6741bc4e1bee884a3
Author: David Turner <dturner@twopensource.com>
Date:   Mon Jan 26 21:11:02 2015 -0500

    Make beautifulsoup4 dep fixed rather than floating
```

We are noticing that pants is compiling java sources before generating the idea project file. 

```
 ./pants idea examples/src/java:: --explain
Goal Execution Order:

bootstrap -> imports -> unpack-jars -> deferred-sources -> gen -> resolve -> compile -> resources -> idea

Goal [TaskRegistrar->Task] Order:

bootstrap [bootstrap-jvm-tools->BootstrapJvmTools_bootstrap_bootstrap_jvm_tools]
imports [ivy-imports->IvyImports_imports_ivy_imports]
unpack-jars [unpack-jars->UnpackJars_unpack_jars]
deferred-sources [deferred-sources->DeferredSourcesMapper_deferred_sources]
gen [thrift->ApacheThriftGen_gen_thrift, scrooge->ScroogeGen_gen_scrooge, protoc->ProtobufGen_gen_protoc, antlr->AntlrGen_gen_antlr, ragel->RagelGen_gen_ragel, jaxb->JaxbGen_gen_jaxb, wire->WireGen_gen_wire, aapt->AaptGen_gen_aapt]
resolve [ivy->IvyResolve_resolve_ivy]
compile [compile->NoopCompile_compile, jvm->SingletonGroupTask_compile_jvm, checkstyle->Checkstyle_compile_checkstyle, scalastyle->Scalastyle_compile_scalastyle]
resources [prepare->PrepareResources_resources_prepare]
idea [idea->IdeaGen_idea]
```

It used to be;

```
Goal Execution Order:

bootstrap -> imports -> gen -> check-exclusives -> resolve -> idea

Goal [TaskRegistrar->Task] Order:

bootstrap [bootstrap-jvm-tools->BootstrapJvmTools_bootstrap_bootstrap_jvm_tools]
imports [ivy-imports->IvyImports_imports_ivy_imports]
gen [thrift->ApacheThriftGen_gen_thrift, scrooge->ScroogeGen_gen_scrooge, protoc->ProtobufGen_gen_protoc, antlr->AntlrGen_gen_antlr, ragel->RagelGen_gen_ragel, jaxb->JaxbGen_gen_jaxb, wire->WireGen_gen_wire, aapt->AaptGen_gen_aapt]
check-exclusives [check-exclusives->CheckExclusives_check_exclusives]
resolve [ivy->IvyResolve_resolve_ivy]
idea [idea->IdeaGen_idea]
```

Using git bisect, it seems that this problem started here:

```
21346944b695e0b14c3154a5af5a15740c5b3e95 is the first bad commit
commit 21346944b695e0b14c3154a5af5a15740c5b3e95
Author: John Sirois <john.sirois@gmail.com>
Date:   Sun Jan 25 23:48:18 2015 -0700

    Improve RoundEngine lifecycle.
```

issue_title: GoRemoteLibrary/GoFetch do not support packages in subdirectories.
issue_body:
Some repos place all their code in one or more subpackages and this trips up GoFetch recusrsive resolution.  Concretely, for a github repo with go code laid out like so:
```
https://github.com/pantsbuild/golibs/
  rpc/
    rpc.go
  io/
    io.go
  util/
    dir.go
    file.go
```
Will fail [here](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/contrib/go/src/python/pants/contrib/go/tasks/go_fetch.py#L201-L202) when GoFetch tries to determine if the repo has its own remote dependencies to fetch.  In general GoFetch needs to recursively glob all go files in the exploded zip package dir.

An example of this sort of repo is here: https://github.com/AdRoll/goamz

issue_title: Split rust engine into more crates
issue_body:
Compilation time of the rust engine snuck up on us over the last few months, such that it now takes 70 seconds to run an optimized build of the engine.

Currently, the entire rust engine is a single crate: breaking into more crates (along reasonable boundaries) would be very likely to help, as cargo is able to parallelize compilation at the crate level.

This is related to #4585, which proposes to split out the `fs.rs` module into a separate crate anyway. cc @jsirois @illicitonion @kwlzn

issue_title: Feature request: enable more modular swagger.yaml and template.yaml
issue_body:
**Goal:** To have more modular code base for my AWS SAM solution


**Current Reality:** Definitions belonging to template.yaml  must be in a single file (e.g., template.yaml). 


**Problems:**
- If we have 50 different Lambda functions, then the template.yaml file is a huge file, so readability quickly decreases 
- If I want my team members to work on different categories of sub features, I can't have them work on different problems without interfering others.

**Feature request (idea for discussion)**:
It would be great if we can have the following directory:

> samdir/template.yaml
>                      /action/action_logic.yaml
>                      /payment/payment_logic.yaml
> 

--- inside template.yaml -----

```
 ...
 ...
 - include {./action/action_logic.yaml}
 - include {./payment/payment_logic.yaml}
```

Actually, the above mentioned capability (to achieve composibility and modularization) is needed for the swagger.yaml file as well for a large project, I think.

issue_title: Add the metadata for FMN in the headers
issue_body:
The new FMN will leverage RabbitMQ's header exchange feature to distribute relevant message in user-specific queues, that would then be distributed by IRC or email.

To use the header exchange, we need the following data in the headers:
* RPM package name that is affected by the action
* FAS username(s) of the user(s) who is/are affected by the action

We could add the username who did the action, but I'm not sure that's really useful. Why would you want to be notified of actions you take, after all? I could be useful for mentoring, though.

The header exchange in RabbitMQ [only does string matching](https://www.rabbitmq.com/tutorials/amqp-concepts.html#exchange-headers), and there can be multiple users affected by a message. A header won't be matched if it's a list, and there can't be multiple headers with the same key.
It does not appear that we'll be able to entirely rely on RabbitMQ's header routing, and avoid a custom daemon. Am I missing something?

In any case, the base `Message` class must have 2 accessors, `package` and `affected_usernames` (names to be debated) that would return `None` and an empty list by default, and that publishers can override.

issue_title: Copy tests for distance from scipy
Update docs to explain our policy on OccupancyType vs SubaddressType
issue_body:
More discussion in [this PR](https://github.com/datamade/usaddress/pull/154) and #132.

Basically, we need a policy for differentiating between `OccupancyType` and `SubaddressType`, since our use of the two tags differs from the URISA standard. From my interpretation of the existing training data, I'm inclined to say that `SubaddressType` divides an address into a **smaller unit** (e.g. an office suite, a mailroom, a floor) while `OccupancyType` defines the **endpoint** of an address (e.g. an apartment, a room).    

This definition introduces some interesting ambiguities: some common tokens (`room`, `floor`) could be `OccupancyType` or `SubaddressType` based on their context. For example, `suite` would normally be an `OccupancyType`, but in the context of `suite 30 room 406` it would become a ` SubaddressType`.

issue_title: Question : How can easily play a song from Google Play Music
issue_body:
Hi folks,

I recently installed the SoCo repo and tried some python command to understand how SoCo works. But I did not succeed to play a song from my Google Play Music account (already paired with my Sonos).

My goal is to use SoCo in an open-source project called Mycroft ([Website](https://mycroft.ai//), [GitHub](https://github.com/MycroftAI/mycroft-core)). I'd like to implement a plugin (called _Skill_) to control Sonos speakers. This _Skill_ will allow to control Sonos speakers by the voice.
My first step is to make a python script that plays song (by author, album, ...) and playlist from a Google Play Music account but I need a little help to understand how to use SoCo properly.

May you introduce me the way ? :-)

issue_title: 404 on Album Art URI
issue_body:
I can't manage to display Album Art using the URI from my music library (not a music service)

I use the following process:
`albums = device.music_library.get_music_library_information('albums',search_term="passant")
`
I get the first album of the list : 
`album = albums[0]`

Then I get the album art URI using 
`aa_uri = album.album_art_uri`

Then I use 
`device.music_library._build_album_art_full_uri(aa_uri)`

I have the following URI as a result:
`http://192.168.1.22:1400/getaa?u=x-file-cifs%3a%2f%2fbelle%2fdownloads%2fmusic%2fJean%2520Jacques%2520Goldman%2f12%2520-%2520En%2520passant%2520(1997)%2f01%2520-%2520Sache%2520que%2520je.mp3&v=503
`
Then I got a 404 page.

issue_title: Update button will not be shown without refreshing the page
issue_body:
### Observed behavior

If there's an updated version of a channel from Kolibri Studio, Kolibri will not show that the channel has a newer version available until the users refresh the page

### Expected behavior

Not sure if this is expected, but I guess it could be better if the users can see there is a newer version available without refreshing the page? Or maybe we can put messages such as "Please refresh the page to see if there's a newer version available" in the channel page?

### User-facing consequences

Users  don't know if there's a newer version available if they don't refresh the page

### Errors and logs

Relevant errors and tracebacks from:

* the command line
* `~/.kolibri/kolibri.log`
* the browser console

```
server logs
```

```
browser console logs
```

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a test channel on Studio and publish it
2. Import the channel to Kolibri
3. Make changes on the channel and publish it again
4. Go back to the channel page on Kolibri to check if the update button is shown to indicate that there's a newer version available. (I tried with waiting for 30 minutes but the update button was still not shown)

### Context

* Kolibri version: 0.7
* Operating system: Mac OS X/ demo server
* Browser: chrome

issue_title: License instances are overwriting each other when being merged into main DB
issue_body:
## Summary

Saw a few cases of the wrong license showing under content. Turns out we use an auto-incrementing ID on License in the Kolibri models, and only export the licenses used in a channel, so the pk's don't always correspond to a particular license. Then, when we merge them into the joint DB, they overwrite each other (or don't, but in any case, many license FK's end up pointing to the wrong license.

In fixing this, we'll need to think holistically across Studio models, the export process, and the import process, and take into account Special Permissions, which come with custom text (so there's more than one Special Permissions license, in effect).

## How to reproduce

1. Import 66cef05505fa550b970e69c3623e82ba and 8b28761bac075deeb66adc6c80ef119c
1. Go to content pages
1. Depending on which channel was loaded first, content from one channel will show the wrong license.

## Screenshots

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/618416/31102718-3c8df5ea-a788-11e7-925a-5d4971d6898d.png)

## Real-life consequences

Boatloads of legal trouble for everyone! :)

issue_title: AuthorizationError with severals accounts
issue_body:
First of all, thanks for your project, sounds really promising. 
Nevertheless for me, it doesn't work, our company is organised in a way that everyone connect to the main account on which there is no resources at all (all operation get denied by default), its just used as a gateway to switch between accounts. (its AWS best practise when an Organisation has severals accounts).

With boto3, its work fine and i do it as follow.

```
cat ~/.aws/credentials
[account1]
aws_access_key_id = ...
aws_secret_access_key = ..
region=us-east-1
role_arn=arn:aws:iam::...

[account2]
aws_access_key_id = ...
aws_secret_access_key = ..
region=us-east-1
role_arn=arn:aws:iam::...
```
example working boto3 starter for s3
```
import boto3

client = boto3.Session()
profiles = client.available_profiles <= return ["account1","account2"]
boto3.setup_default_session(profile_name='account1')
s3 = boto3.resource('s3')
for bucket in s3.buckets.all():
...     print bucket.name
bucket1
bucket2
etc...
```

This snippet works fine and i successfully retrieve any kind of informations from "account1" or "account2"

So with your program, i tried the following command line, for example s3

```
python Scout2.py --profile account1 --services s3
```
and it returns me for every single operation

```
Error: could not fetch s3 configuration.
An error occurred (AccessDenied) when calling the ListBuckets operation: Access Denied
```

While i do have all perms since i succesfully retrieve them using boto3 lib. So i'am unsure where is the problem, but i would love to be able to try your program, may be could be a quick fix ? In anycase, if you think its a bug, i can help you out with it for testing. 

Regards

Steve

issue_title: Extra package needed
issue_body:
I installed Scout2 through pip and when I tried to run it the following message appeared:

```bash
$ Scout2 --help                                                                                                                              
Error: Scout2 depends on the opinel package. Install all the requirements with the following command:
  $ pip install -r requirements.txt
'module' object has no attribute 'SSL_ST_INIT'
```

Strangely, the `opinel` package was already installed :/
Googling for the error message I found that I didn't have the pyOpenSSL package installed. So after doing:

```bash
$ sudo -H pip install --upgrade pyOpenSSL
```

Scout2 started working \o/

Don't know if this is a fault on `opinel` or `Scout2` for not pulling `pyOpenSSL` during the installation via `pip` :/

This is the info of the machine I'm using:

```
DISTRIB_ID=LinuxMint
DISTRIB_RELEASE=18.2
DISTRIB_CODENAME=sonya
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Linux Mint 18.2 Sonya"

Linux thinkpad 4.8.0-53-generic #56~16.04.1-Ubuntu SMP Tue May 16 01:18:56 UTC 2017 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

Anyway, I tested Scout2 and it gave a very interesting report :smile: 
Keep doing the good work :+1:

issue_title: -r requirements.txt parameters should be implicit

issue_title: django.core.exceptions.ImproperlyConfigured: Django 1.11 is not supported.
Subsequent runs of ipwb may use stale cdxj
issue_body:
We currently store the current CDXJ path in ~/.ipfs/config. In some instances that are difficult to replicate, starting a new ipwb session will use the previous session's CDXJ. I encountered this when starting a session w/ the 5mementos warc but still receiving acrts.warc.gz mementos in the replay UI.

For this ticket:
- [ ] Investigate why, when, and where we are using this value from ~/.ipfs/config in the code.
- [ ] Be able to replicate this bug.
- [ ] Remedy this issue.

issue_title: charm-build should error if defining options for a non-existant layer
issue_body:
When defining layer options according to JSON Schema, such as the following [link describes](http://spacetelescope.github.io/understanding-json-schema/reference/generic.html): i would expect any inheriting layer, that defines an option that doesn't exist in the list to error during  `charm build`

Given in the base layer:

```yaml
defines:
  storage-driver:
    enum: [overlay, aufs, btrfs, zfs]
    default: aufs
    description: |
      Used in conjunction with the storage volume in metadata.yaml.
      Supported providers, when prompted, will allow you to mount a block
      device, formatted for use with the storage driver configured here.
```

And in the inheriting layer:

```yaml
options:
  storage-driver:
    value: schenanigans
```

I expected during the build time, the base schema to be validated. ENUM type should have limited the values to those defined as valid: 'aufs', 'btrfs', 'zfs', or 'overlay' - anything outside of this pre-defined list when provided as a value should have actively prevented the build, or failed it, as it doesn't validate against the defined schema.

issue_title: multiple layers that inherit layer-basic gets downloaded multiple times
issue_body:
An example output of my ghost layer which inherits a few layers which all inherit the base layer as their starting point:

http://paste.ubuntu.com/13503577/

Lines 19, 21, 23 show the build process cloning the base layer multiple times. This doesn't break anything but does add an additional wait time when building out the layers

issue_title: Migrate from interfaces.juju.solutions to a GitHub repo
issue_body:
The layer index site works, but was originally intended to be an interim to finding a more permanent solution.   It has not, however, been improved or replaced.  It is also confusing for users in that it is separate from both the charm store and the code base.  Instead of maintaining an active index site & API, we should use a GitHub repo to manage the layer index.  This would allow us to use PRs for managing the index, giving more visibility to the community when layers are added, modified, or removed.

I think we should probably have a YAML file per record in the current database, and we might want to include some script or hook for updating a Markdown index (perhaps a subsection of the README.md) with clickable links to the repos referenced in the YAML files.

issue_title: getting None from active()
issue_body:
I'm trying to use 'active()' to find active firewall in my HA cluster. My cluster members have IPs '10.34.2.21' and '10.34.2.20'. Currently '10.34.2.21' is active
```
$ cat pandevice.fw.commit_all.py
#!/usr/bin/python
from pandevice import base
from pandevice import firewall
from pandevice import panorama
from pandevice import policies
from pandevice import objects
from pandevice import network
from pandevice import device
fw = base.PanDevice('10.34.2.21', 'admin', 'password',vsys='vsys1')
print fw.active()
```

But no matter what cluster member I test, I got 'None' in response ?

```
$ python pandevice.fw.commit_all.py
None
```

issue_title: JSON encoding a BinaryQuadraticModel results in quadratic labels being reversed
issue_body:
https://github.com/dwavesystems/dimod/blob/c17f8f36ad91a12a580d12afeceaaab6afafdf74/dimod/io/json.py#L120-L121

These lines cause the following behavior:

```python
>>> bqm = dimod.BinaryQuadraticModel({0: 1, 1: 1}, {(0, 1): -1.0}, 0.0, dimod.SPIN)
>>> dimod.BinaryQuadraticModel.from_json(bqm.to_json())
BinaryQuadraticModel({0: 1, 1: 1}, {(1, 0): -1.0}, 0.0, Vartype.SPIN)
```

Note that the quadratic has been changed from `(0, 1)` to `(1, 0)`.

issue_title: ptyprocess fails on OpenIndiana 151a9 and Python 2.7.11 when the process lacks a controlling terminal
issue_body:
pexpect is failing in ptyprocess.spawn on OpenIndiana 151a9 (a distro of Illumos/OpenSolaris). pexpect is 4.1.0, ptyprocess is 0.5.1:

    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "/home/majid/bin/upload_file.py", line 26, in <module>
        upload(fn)
      File "/home/majid/bin/upload_file.py", line 7, in upload
        timeout=14400)
      File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pexpect/pty_spawn.py", line 198, in __init__
        self._spawn(command, args, preexec_fn, dimensions)
      File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pexpect/pty_spawn.py", line 298, in _spawn
        cwd=self.cwd, **kwargs)
      File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pexpect/pty_spawn.py", line 309, in _spawnpty
        return ptyprocess.PtyProcess.spawn(args, **kwargs)
      File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/ptyprocess/ptyprocess.py", line 223, in spawn
        pid, fd = _fork_pty.fork_pty()
      File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/ptyprocess/_fork_pty.py", line 30, in fork_pty
        pty_make_controlling_tty(child_fd)
      File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/ptyprocess/_fork_pty.py", line 76, in pty_make_controlling_tty
        fd = os.open("/dev/tty", os.O_WRONLY)
    OSError: [Errno 6] No such device or address: '/dev/tty'

The script is run under the control of `at(1)`, and the job itself is queued from a crontab. It looks like what is happening is that when the parent process, which has no controlling terminal, calls os.openpty(), it acquires the pty as a controlling terminal. The problem seems to occur when posix_openpty pushes the ptem STREAMS module onto the slave pty fd, the simple call `ioctl(slave_fd, I_PUSH, "ptem")` causes the parent process to acquire the pty as its controlling terminal, and thus the child, which is in a different session, is unable to acquire it and the failure at line 76 in _fork_pty().

I differed posixmodule.c on Python 3.5 vs 2.7.11, the implementation of posix_openpty is not much different.

Older versions of pexpect had a different implementation _svr4_openpty(), but those look very similar to the Python posixmodule.c code.

issue_title: calling nidcpower measure_multiple causes python to crash
issue_body:
Calling nidcpower measure_multiple causes python environment to crash if there is a method with session.get_channel_name existed before .
 replace dcpower systemtests as follows.

```
import nidcpower
import pytest
import re


@pytest.fixture(scope='function')
def session():
    with nidcpower.Session('FakeDevice', '', False, 'Simulate=1, DriverSetup=Model:4143; BoardType:PXIe') as simulated_session:
        yield simulated_session


def test_get_channel_name(session):
    name = session.get_channel_name(1)
    assert name == '0'


def test_measure_multiple(session):
    session.source_mode = nidcpower.SourceMode.SINGLE_POINT
    session.measure_when = nidcpower.MeasureWhen.ON_DEMAND
    with session.initiate():
        voltage_measurements, current_measurements = session.measure_multiple()
        assert len(voltage_measurements) == 4   # measuremultiple will return a reading for all channel , since 4143 has 4 channel expecting 4 readings 
        assert len(current_measurements) == 4 
        assert isinstance(voltage_measurement[1], float)  
        assert isinstance(current_measurements[1], float) 



Also noticed that assertion on test_get_attribute_string pass only if run after test_get_channel_name test. if not session.instrument_model is returning as 4110 .

Are we simulating the device right way ? should we do a reset while invoking session ?

issue_title: Select2 fails to load
issue_body:
I've just upgraded an old django project from 1.3 -> 1.8 and am trying to add django-advanced-features to replace an old custom filtering application.

I've installed django_advanced_filters 1.0.5, and django_easy_select2 1.2.5. I've followed the installation guide and successfully added the `AdminAdvancedFiltersMixin` to one of my models. I can see the admin templates are loading correctly but I seem to be getting JS errors related to select2.

E.g. on `http://localhost:8080/admin/advanced_filters/advancedfilter/2/` I see:

```
Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read property 'fn' of undefined
    at select2.min.js:21
    at select2.min.js:21
```

And on my model changelist page (`http://localhost:8080/admin/mymodel/`):

```
select2.min.js:21 Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read property 'fn' of undefined
    at select2.min.js:21
    at select2.min.js:21

advanced-filters.js:140 Uncaught TypeError: $(...).select2 is not a function
    at HTMLInputElement.<anonymous> (advanced-filters.js:140)
    at Function.each (jquery.min.js:2)
    at m.fn.init.each (jquery.min.js:2)
    at OperatorHandlers.self.destroy (advanced-filters.js:139)
    at HTMLDocument.<anonymous> (advanced-filters.js:150)
    at j (jquery.min.js:2)
    at Object.fireWith [as resolveWith] (jquery.min.js:2)
    at Function.ready (jquery.min.js:2)
    at HTMLDocument.J (jquery.min.js:2)
```

This looks to me like some kind of jquery load order issue, and I'm aware django does some funny things with various versions, but I'm a bit stuck on how to debug. Can anyone point me in the right direction? I did wonder if it's due to the several difference versions of jquery loading and re-initialising `$`, but I believe django does some clever stuff to namespace the versions. In case it's helpful here are the `.js` files loading on my model changelist page:

```
jsi18n/	200	script	(index)	2.7 KB	246 ms

issue_title: Add ability to merge JSON objects together.
issue_body:
Use case:

Given
```
{"a": {"key1": "val1", "key2": "val2"}, "key3": "val3", "b": {"key4": "val4"}
```

I'd like to create:

```
{"key1": "val1", "key2": "val2", "key3": "val3", "key4": "val4"}
```

In python it's the equivalent of:

```
d = {}
d.update(current['a'])
d['key3'] = current['key3']
d.update(current['b'])
```

Possible syntax:

  1. Add a merge function.  It would variadic and take multiple JSON objects, and produce a merged JSON object.

```
merge(a, {key3: key3}, b)
```

  2. Add syntax to multiselect-hash.  We could borrow python's `**kwargs`:

```
{**a, key3: key3, **b}
```

issue_title: Several Endpoint types can't autoactivate, may be a problem for hosted CLI transition
issue_body:
There's at least a couple of types of activation other than S3 endpoints which can be done via the hosted CLI, but which we can't replicate.

GSI-SSH credentials aren't going to be supported.
Do we have any fallback for endpoints which may still be using `endpoint-activate -g`?
If not, the hosted CLI may need to drop `endpoint-activate -g` support prior to the transition.

MyProxy-activation can be done directly from the CLI.
At the very least, there's the possibility of someone scripting with `echo "..." | ssh ... endpoint-activate ...`
It also seems that there may be scenarios in which the MyProxy server may issue a fresh proxy cert without requiring username/password? I have one user story that suggests this to be the case, but cannot verify at present.

For MyProxy endpoints, should we consider reintroducing `globus endpoint activate`, consuming myproxy credentials?

cc @ranantha

---

These are the activation types which we need to make decisions about:

issue_title: UTF-16 without BOM detection
issue_body:
I have a sample file which I downloaded, which is UTF-16LE and/or UCS-2LE - every second byte is 0.  From my guesses, this is a file that has been produced by a Java program prior to Java 1.5, and it has not written the BOM to the head of the file.  Another candidate could be Windows prior to Windows 2000, which evidently did not write BOMs either.

This file gets detected to be "ascii".  Thus I think making a guess at this being UTF-16LE should be fairly trivial.  

If we get to the end of processing (if we did not encounter a BOM mark), if we still think it is ASCII, if char[position modulo 2] is '\0' with high probability (e.g. greater than 95%), then it is likely to be UTF-16LE.  Equally, writing a detector for UTF-16BE is trivially the inverse.

Now one thing to note is that one could get a false-positive detection of UTF-16 if it were UTF-32, so I think you'd want a combined UTF-16 & UTF-32 detector, and the UTF-32 detector would be built around the same principles.

Lastly, this test case shows the ASCII prediction is wrong.  Numbers clearly outside what one would regard as ASCII (e.g. 50% of the file is the null character) are present.

If there's somewhere useful for me to add my test-case input file, I can make a PR to add it to the repo, as a test case of a file that is interesting to detect, for which we currently are not successfully detecting.

issue_title: Make sure we use a ProcessPoolExecutor for cpu-intensive tasks
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

@carver [run into some issues when using a ProcessPoolExecutor](https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/pull/754) as the default executor, and apparently [GvR thinks that's not a good idea anyway](https://groups.google.com/d/msg/python-tulip/91NCCqV4SFs/9McZnfea_VcJ) so we're back to using a ThreadPoolExecutor as the default

### How can it be fixed

Places where we use loop.run_in_executor to offload cpu-intensive tasks must explicitly use a ProcessPoolExecutor

issue_title: Trinity should relay transactions
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

Currently we do nothing when people send us transactions.

### How can it be fixed

Basic transaction relaying should be a good minimal first step towards a transaction pool.

1. A new long running service, `TransactionPool`
2. The pool should maintain something like a `Queue` where batches of transactions may be placed.
3. The pool should maintain individual bloom filters for each peer to track what transactions we've sent to each peer.

When we receive transactions, push them onto the `TransactionPool` queue.  Ensure that we update the bloom filter for the peer who sent us the transactions with the newly received transactions.

In the main loop of the `TransactionPool` service:

- pull a batch of transactions from the queue.
- for each peer, filter the transactions to only ones that have not been transmitted to that peer and send them the transactions.

issue_title: Improve error messaging when not matching function is found.
issue_body:
* Version: 4.0.0

link: https://github.com/ethereum/web3.py/issues/752

### What was wrong?

The error messaging when no matching function can be found is not as helpful as it could be, providing no contextual information about what went wrong or how to fix it.

```python
/lib/python3.6/site-packages/web3/utils/contracts.py in find_matching_fn_abi(abi, fn_identifier, args, kwargs)
     98         return function_candidates[0]
     99     if not function_candidates:
--> 100         raise ValueError("No matching functions found")
    101     else:
    102         raise ValueError("Multiple functions found")

ValueError: No matching functions found
```

### How can it be fixed?

This error message should be updated to contain the following.

- what function name was called
- what argument *types* were provided
- *if* no name match was found, a list of available function names.
- *if* multiple name matches were found, the type signatures for those functions.
- *if* an exact name match was found but the type signatures didn't match, the expected type signatures.

Hi,

I have a custom channel on a private server, added in `.condarc` as 
`http:USER:PASSWD@server.url`.

It has been working for a long time, but now it says:

`WARNING: The remote server could not find the noarch directory for the
requested channel with url: https://conda.anaconda.org/http:USER:PASSWD@server.url`.

Of course the `noarch/repodata.json` and associated `noarch/repodata.json.bz2` files are there, as have always been.

I feel it is strange the `conda` url is prepended to my custom url, what's going on?

issue_title: Audit open() and other os.create type calls to replace stack trace with CondaError
noarch only repositories do not work as expected
issue_body:
A http based conda repository with only:

```
/channel-name
   /noarch
      repodata.json
      [packages]
```

Will not work for _any_ platform, as the sub-folder for a given os-bits (win-64/osx-64 etc) [must exist as a requirement](https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/972ad5d5f6d4c987d683aa6bedfbed3ba7753c5c/conda/fetch.py#L107).  The workaround we use currently is to make empty folders for the supported platforms with empty `repodata.json` and `.index.json`, but it feels hacky:

```
/channel-name
   /noarch
      repodata.json
      packages ...
   /win-64
      repodata.json
      [no packages]
```

In the related fetch code [here](https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/972ad5d5f6d4c987d683aa6bedfbed3ba7753c5c/conda/fetch.py#L124) if the "noarch" folder missing it is ignored silently, can we do a similar thing for platform-bits?  I am happy to file a PR on this if that is an acceptable solution.  It add's the capacity for silent failures of repositories, which does not feel good.  Perhaps provide a list of failed repo-fetches at the end?  From this angle, I can see some clear reasons that noarch needs to be treated specially.  

Similarly I also think it seems sane to treat the noarch and platform folders similarly, as the ideal behavior is basically merging the noarch and platform package list at a later step.

See also:
https://github.com/conda/conda-build/issues/462

issue_title: overwritten file in conda env create
issue_body:
Hello,

When doing:
```
conda env create -n myenv -f env1.yml -f env2.yml
```

`conda` silently installs only what's defined in `env2.yml` and nothing of what is specified in `env1.yml`. 
A solution is:
```
conda env create -n myenv -f env1.yml
conda env update -n myenv -f env2.yml
```

However the behaviour of `conda env create` as used in the first example is confusing. Either you want:
* the `conda env create -h` command explain this behaviour
* or install at the same time what's specified with all occurrences of `-f` in the command

Best regards,
Sebastian

issue_title: conda libraries clobber logging configuration
issue_body:
```py
>>> import logging
>>> logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
>>> print(logging.getLogger().level)
20
>>> import conda.base.context
>>> print(logging.getLogger().level)
40
```

I tracked this down to `conda.common.io.attach_stderr_handler`.  

Could this kind of forcible logging configuration be moved to `conda.cli` or somewhere else that is not imported when conda is used as a library?

BTW all I was trying to do was get the path to conda's build directory programatically.

issue_title: Optional dependencies and features as ways to segment examples, source, data, tests, docs
issue_body:
It feels like many conda packages could be decomposed into a subset of components that come from these groups:

* binaries (pre-compiled)
* docs
* examples
* tests
* data
* source

In many cases it may be desirable to have just one of these (e.g. docs, or binaries, or examples), whereas in others some collection of those may be appropriate.

It would be nice to consider what the preferred patterns are to create this kind of decomposition, leveraging either (or both) optional dependencies and features.  I do not immediately have a suggestion for this, but as I watch the output of `--debug` and see lots of package contents being written to disk that I have no interest in it is making me think of ways to address this.

issue_title: Provide method to get an OrderedDict from a section

Windows CI using http://www.appveyor.com/
Time complexity of AlignmentFile class members references and lengths
issue_body:
Recently I noticed very slow parsing of a BAM file with 500k references, which I thought pretty mysterious. 

The loop iterated over, in part, one of either `AlignmentFile.references` and `AlignmentFile.lengths`. The other was being dereferenced inside the loop.

Eg.
```
for n, reflen in enumerate(bam.lengths):
    refname = bam.references[n]
   ...
```
As these appear as arrays in pysam documentation, I thought this would be O(1) but it clearly was not. Inspecting , libcalignmentfile.pyx I guess the answer is that tuples are built _every call_!

Could the documentation be amended or better yet can memoization be applied to the Cython methods?

issue_title: Too many management API calls
issue_body:
For a while now we've been having bad throttling issues, where things
kept crashing with errors.  After a long debugging session where I went
into the code to track where the bad calls are made, I saw that the
offending calls are ones that are made to the management API, eg:

    https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/[...]/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts?api-version=2017-06-01

Later on, we found that it's no wonder that we've been throttled, since
apparently the limit for these calls is 100 per 5 minutes, which is easy
to reach when we have a bunch of blobs to delete, which we have to do
one by one (see Issue #3757).  To make things worse, it looks like this
limit is at the subscription level, and therefore it's even easier to
reach.

Digging more into the code, it looked like these calls are made to get
the storage access key, so we've tried providing that (via
`$AZURE_STORAGE_KEY`) and the throttling problems went away.  To
clarify, this became a bad blocking issue for us since we got to a point
where we couldn't create any new builds.

I also see that the help text claims that `--account-name` must be used
with `--account-key`, which we haven't followed.

So: I think that as a good short-term stopgap it would be best to clear
the help text and say that it will retrieve the key, but that makes for
another API call and therefore it slows things down and it might run
into the much-tighter throttling limits on the management service.

A better long-term solution would be to cache the access key itself,
possibly together with the cached login information.  For example, it
would retrieve and cache the key if it's not there, and if it uses a
cached key and that doesn't work, only then try to re-retrieve the key
in case it changed.  This would make it possible to lift the requirement
of using both the account name and the key, and still have a sane
interaction.

issue_title: The pains in copying web contents
issue_body:
(This is basically an extended feature request list.  To skip to it, see
under the bad.)

I've been hacking on some script that copies a bunch of web pages
(generated api documentation pages) to blob storage, which is possible,
but painful.  What I wanted to do is:

1. Use proper content types
2. Serve a default html file when visiting `.../foo/` instead of forcing
   `.../foo/index.html`
3. Optionally have generated default index pages when such a file does
   not exist.
4. Make `.../foo` redirect the browser to `.../foo/` which would then be
   the above default page.

The way that I achieved all of this is with a pile of hacks:

1. Iterate over a manually-made list of glob patterns and matching
   content types, using `--pattern` to copy only the respective subset
   of files with a correct type.  (This needs to be done carefully,
   keeping account of paths in case new file types are added in the
   future.)
2. For each case where there's a directory with an `index.html` file,
   copy that file to a blob name that looks like `.../dirname/`.
3. A manual pre-scan of the whole directory generates a simple
   `index.html` in directories that don't have one.
4. Finally, for each directory `foo/bar` I upload an html file that is
   called `foo/bar` with a
       <meta http-equiv="refresh" content="0; url=bar/" />
   which works, except that it makes the browser show the stub html for
   a brief moment before jumping on (and it also doesn't work with
   things like `curl`).

Note that this means one `az` use for each type, and two more for each
directory, which means a significant overall time, almost completely
spent in the ~2 second overhead for each such use!

---

My feature requests are therefore:

* A way to specify "use the right content type based on file names"
* A way to serve some default sub-blob for requests for `foo/bar/` paths
  that end with a slash.
* I know that dealing with the redirections is too big for a quick
  feature request, but it would be much better if the [list of http properties](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/storageservices/setting-and-retrieving-properties-and-metadata-for-blob-resources#a-namesubheading3a-standard-http-properties-for-containers-and-blobs)
  that can be set on blobs would include `Location`, and the existence
  of that header would also make the blob serve with a 307 status.  This
  will even make it a good substitute for creating "symlink"-like blobs.
  (It could also be used for serving a default file.)

  If this was implemented as part of the above "use the right type", it would
  be a significant step towards making it possible to host html content in
  blob storage.

issue_title: List subscriptions with user's role
issue_body:
We are using azure-cli for our automation scripts, and we would like to get a list of all subscriptions that user is an owner of (or an administrator).
 
We couldn't find any easy way to do it, so we would do it as follow:

1. `az account list` and retrieved an email from `user.name`

2. Retrieve user's objectId by `az ad user list --query "[?mail=='<EMAIL>']"`

3. And finally execute `az role assignment list --query "[?principalId=='<OBJECT_ID>']"` to get a role.

The main issue is that in some cases in the 2. step, not every user has a mail assigned to it (why?).
Also, all these steps are a bit cumbersome, especially if a user has lots of subscriptions. I hope there is a better way.

To summarise, is there a way to retrieve current user's objectId or list all subscriptions with user's role(s) in it?

-rw-r--r-- 1 ubuntu ubuntu 31G Jul  9 15:35 image.vhd
$ du -h image.vhd 
1015M	image.vhd
```

Running

```
az storage blob upload <snip> --type page --file image.vhd --name image.vhd
```

takes substantially longer than uploading a file that contains only the non-sparse parts of the image. This strongly suggests to me that (a) the upload isn't taking advantage of the file's sparseness, and (b) the file as stored in Azure won't be sparse (making operation on it more expensive in the future).

**To Reproduce**
Create a sparse file and upload it.

**Expected behavior**
The upload would take the amount of time that uploading the non-sparse part of the file takes, and would be stored as sparse in Azure.

**Environment summary**

pip installed on Ubuntu 18.10

Here is the culprit:
```
$ py.test tests/commands/install_uninstall_test.py -k test_environment_not_sourced                
========================================================================== test session starts ===========================================================================
platform linux2 -- Python 2.7.6 -- py-1.4.26 -- pytest-2.6.4
plugins: ipdb, xdist
collected 24 items 

tests/commands/install_uninstall_test.py F

================================================================================ FAILURES ================================================================================
______________________________________________________________________ test_environment_not_sourced ______________________________________________________________________

tmpdir_factory = <tests.conftest.TmpdirFactory object at 0x7f0a4cfa3890>

    def test_environment_not_sourced(tmpdir_factory):
        path = make_consuming_repo(tmpdir_factory, 'script_hooks_repo')
        with cwd(path):
            # Patch the executable to simulate rming virtualenv
            with mock.patch.object(sys, 'executable', '/bin/false'):
                assert install(Runner(path)) == 0
    
            ret, stdout, stderr = cmd_output(
                'git', 'commit', '--allow-empty', '-m', 'foo',
                env={'HOME': os.path.expanduser('~')},
                retcode=None,

>           assert ret == 1
E           assert 0 == 1

tests/commands/install_uninstall_test.py:170: AssertionError

It looks like `mock.patch.object(sys, 'executable', '/bin/false')` is not enough: `pre-commit` can still be found as an executable in the path or a python lib by `.git/hooks/pre-commit`. That's probably because I installed it with with `pip install --user` and not used any virtualenv.

I'd suggest just removing this test as the balance usefulness / complexity to make it pass does not look interesting to me. What kind of regression could it catch anyway ?

issue_title: Add hook option to not pass matching files as arguments to the program
issue_body:
It would be useful to add some hook option to _**not**_ pass matching files as arguments to the program, something like `- filenames-as-args: false`.

This would enable, for instance, easier integration of pre-commit into a repository using Makefiles or similar tools that find the required files to check by themselves and do not accept them in command line.

Currently, I'm using the following workaround to call `make check` to build and test C/C++ code:

- Defining the hook to call a wrapper script that forwards to `make`only the arguments before some delimiter, such as `--`.
- Adding a delimiter at the end of hook args.

However, forcing the user to add this delimiter whenever she wants to customize `make` arguments is confusing and error-prone. I'd like to write `args: [check]` instead of `args: [check, --]` and get rid of the wrapper script.

issue_title: Maintain compatibility between yarl and aiohttp
issue_body:
## Long story short

After recent upgrade of `yarl` our build that uses `aiohttp==2.2.3` broke with `unexpected keyword argument 'strict' being passed from http parser`. Upon investigation turned out that `yarl>=0.11` is required, but a breaking change was introduced in `0.14`.

## Expected behaviour

One of the following:
1) During setup install the version of `yarl` that maintains a contract with the version of aiohttp being installed.
2) Raise depreciation warning when arguments are being removed in minor version update.

## Actual behaviour

`>=` requirement combined with a breaking change introduced in minor version update resulted in aiohttp being unable to handle any request.

## Steps to reproduce

Running unit tests on 2.2.3 should reproduce the issue.

## Your environment

OSX, aiohttp 2.2.3

issue_title: Suggestion: Use localstack for testing
issue_body:
https://bitbucket.org/atlassian/localstack
This project emulates AWS APIs in the HTTP server level instead of mocking API client methods.
We could write more realistic test cases using this, though I haven't tried this personally yet.

issue_title: 'Full credit'  and 'No credit' buttons on grading bar
issue_body: In addition to being able to add the numerical score, there should be two buttons that assign full credit and no credit. Default tabbing to the numerical score still makes sense though.

issue_title: Validation fails for `assignment_list/main`
issue_body:
In preparation for courses next week, I'm upgrading and testing our jupyter/nbgrader installation.  The following error occurs:
```
$ nbgrader extension activate assignment_list
Unrecognized JSON config file version, assuming version 1

Enabling tree extension assignment_list/main...

Done. You may need to restart the Jupyter notebook server for changes to take effect.
```

Symptoms:  The command-line `nbgrader` works, but the browser extension doesn't appear.  (I'm not worried about the unrecognized JSON version lines—those have always been there without affecting behavior.)

This seems similar to issues #497, #501, and *maybe* #515.

I am using versions:
```
jupyter==1.0.0
jupyter-client==4.3.0
jupyter-console==5.0.0
jupyter-core==4.1.1
nbgrader==0.3.0
notebook==4.2.2
```

Any idea what's gone wrong here?

issue_title: Developer install instructions use old method of installing extensions
issue_body: They should be updated to use `jupyter nbextension install`, etc. See the regular installation instructions for what should be put in the developer installation instructions.

issue_title: [Feature Request] Setting export configuration in frontend
issue_body:
Currently, when using `File->Print Preview` or `Download as` from the notebook, the current document is exported with exporter configuration set by the notebook server configuration.
Changing the configuration requires restarting the notebook server or an explicit call of `nbconvert`.

Allowing to supply configuration options to the exporter from the frontend would allow customizing notebook export on-the-fly. This would also be very useful when using different kernels than Python.

A very simple implementation could use the existing frontend ConfigManager infrastructure.
On the server side, this would only be a few new lines.

issue_title: Support for virtual adversarial perturbations
issue_body:
Foolbox currently only supports finding adversarial examples on samples that are correctly classified to begin with. If one tries to run an attack on an incorrectly classified sample, the following warning results:

>Not running the attack because the original image is already misclassified and the adversarial thus has a distance of 0.

However, in some contexts (such as Virtual Adversarial Training), it is useful to find adversarial examples on incorrectly classified inputs as well. That is, the training label is completely discarded and one instead only focuses on the label assigned by the classifier. These examples are called "virtual adversarial examples". Would it be possible to add support for them?

issue_title: Detect when there are variables within a translatable string
issue_body:
As stated in the [Odoo documentation](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/11.0/reference/translations.html#variables) expressions like the following should be avoided:
```
_("Scheduled meeting with %s" % invitee.name)
```

Instead, dynamic variables should be outside of the translation lookup:
```
_("Scheduled meeting with %s") % invitee.name
```

There could be a lint to prevent this.

issue_title: There is a series of bugs on getting certificate of an app[andguard v2.0]
issue_body:
Hi author and all,
I used millions of apps to verified there is series of bugs on getting certificate info. regarding methods in apk.py are as follow:
get_signature_name
get_signature
show_Certificate

This is because some apps are signed with multi-certificates(different certificates). Even if only one certificate, it may be a certificate chain.

for get_signature_name and get_signature

    def get_signature_name(self):
        signature_expr = re.compile("^(META-INF/)(.*)(\.RSA|\.DSA)$")
        for i in self.get_files():
            if signature_expr.search(i):
                return i
        return None

    def get_signature(self):
        signature_expr = re.compile("^(META-INF/)(.*)(\.RSA|\.DSA)$")
        for i in self.get_files():
            if signature_expr.search(i):
                return self.get_file(i)
        return None

so we need to return the signature_name_list and signature_list

for show_Certificate
we need to loop the all certificates and certificate chain then return or show the info, our solution for this  as follows for you reference:

    def getAllCertInfo(self):
        allCertInfoList = list()
        sigLocatonList = self.__getSignatureNameList()
        for sigLocaton in sigLocatonList:
            success, cert = self.__getCKCert(sigLocaton)
            if success:
                certChain = cert.GetCertChain()
                certChainLen = certChain.get_NumCerts()
                for i in range(0, certChainLen):
                    self.logger.info("signature:%s, num:%s, index:%s" % (sigLocaton, certChainLen, i))
                    c = certChain.GetCert(i)
                    allCertInfoList.append(self.__getCertInfo(c))
            else:
                self.logger.error("get cert by getCKCert error.")
        return allCertInfoList
    
    def __getSignatureNameList(self):
        signatureList = list()
        signature_expr = re.compile("^(META-INF/)(.*)(\.RSA|\.DSA)$")
        for i in self.a.get_files():
            if signature_expr.search(i):
                signatureList.append(i)
        return signatureList
    
    def __getCKCert(self, filename):
        """
            Return a certificate object by giving the name in the apk file
        """
        cert = chilkat.CkCert()
        f = self.a.get_file(filename)
        data = chilkat.CkByteData()
        data.append2(f, len(f))
        success = cert.LoadFromBinary(data)
        return success, cert
    
    def __getCertInfo(self, cert):
        """
            Return a certificate info by giving the name in the apk file
        """
        
        certInfo = dict()
        
        certInfo['issuerC'] = cert.issuerC()
        certInfo['issuerCN'] = cert.issuerCN()

issue_title: Incompatibility with fish shell.
issue_body:
When using the fish shell, sshuttle doesn't work without adding '--python python' to the commandline.  This is due to the '||' in pycmd in ssh.py.
The error is:
```
Unsupported use of '||'. In fish, please use 'COMMAND; or COMMAND'.
fish: P=python3.5; $P -V 2>/dev/null || P=python; exec "$P" -c 'import sys; verbosity=0; stdin=getattr(sys.stdin,"buffer",sys.stdin); exec(compile(stdin.read(915), "assembler.py", "exec"))'
                                      ^
client: fatal: expected server init string 'SSHUTTLE0001'; got ''
```

This is because fish is not fully POSIX compliant and ssh.py makes assumptions about the shell rather than explicitly calling /bin/sh to ensure compatibility.
Here is a patch that works for me:
```
--- ssh.py.orig	2015-12-14 17:36:49.000000000 -0800
+++ ssh.py	2016-03-08 15:08:16.000000000 -0800
@@ -108,8 +108,8 @@
         if python:
             pycmd = "'%s' -c '%s'" % (python, pyscript)
         else:
-            pycmd = ("P=python3.5; $P -V 2>/dev/null || P=python; "
-                     "exec \"$P\" -c '%s'") % pyscript
+            pycmd = ("/bin/sh -c \'P=python3.5; $P -V 2>/dev/null || P=python; "
+                     "exec \"$P\" -c \\'%s\\'\'") % pyscript
         argv = (sshl +
                 portl +
                 [rhost, '--', pycmd])
```

issue_title: Investigate a more flexible way of storing additional order fees
issue_body:
For shops that add extra charges to orders (eg a charge to use a particular payment method), there isn't a natural place to store this field.  You could add fields to the order model but this also requires changes to how order totals are calculated.

It might be better to have a new model for additional charges.  This could be used for shipping charges too and would easily extend for other types of charge.

See https://groups.google.com/forum/#!msg/django-oscar/yTiIMtySxY8/DuxBz-_6f_gJ

issue_title: Referencing a .NET Standard class library from a .NET Core Web App fails during runtime
issue_body:
# Summary
Referencing a .NET Standard 1.4 or 1.6 class library from a .NET Core 1.1 Web App or a .NET Standard 2.0 class library from a .NET Core 2.0 Web App fails at runtime. 

For the .NET Core 1.1 Web App and .NET Standard 1.4 and 1.6 class libraries I've used Visual Studio 2017 Enterprise version 15.2 (26430.15). For the .NET Core 2.0 Web App and the .NET Standard 2.0 class library I've, used Visual Studio Enterprise 2017 version 15.3.0 preview 6.0.

I'm running on Windows 8.1 Enterprise.

Referencing a .NET Standard 1.0 class library from a .NET Core 1.0 Web App works perfectly fine. 

# Steps to reproduce
In both cases I have created a class library targeting .NET Standard. The class library contains one static class with a static method returning a string. 

```cs
using System;

namespace ClassLibrary
{
    public static class TextProvider
    {
        public static string GetGreeting()
        {
            return "Hello from class library!";
        }
    }
}
```

The web app is created using Visual Studios built-in template for a web project. In the "About" action in the HomeController the static method from the class library is used to get the string and displays it in the view. 

```cs
using System.Diagnostics;
using ClassLibrary;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc;
using WebProject.Models;

namespace WebProject.Controllers
{
    public class HomeController : Controller
    {
        public IActionResult Index()
        {
            return View();
        }

        public IActionResult About()
        {
            ViewData["Message"] = TextProvider.GetGreeting();

            return View();
        }

        public IActionResult Contact()
        {
            ViewData["Message"] = "Your contact page.";

            return View();
        }

        public IActionResult Error()
        {
            return View(new ErrorViewModel { RequestId = Activity.Current?.Id ?? HttpContext.TraceIdentifier });
        }
    }
}
```

# Exceptions
For the .NET Core 1.1 case this is the exception thrown when loading the "About" page:

issue_title: Feature request: parameter to list allowed TLDs
issue_body:
Hi,

on automated systems with a lot of domains a single unsupported TLD (like dotbit) causes an error with the need to manage the affected bunch of domains manually.

```
An unexpected error occurred:
The request message was malformed :: Error creating new authz :: Name does not end in a public suffix
```

Please add a parameter to the ACME client which lists the allowed TLDs in a script-comptatible style  to pre-filter allowed TLDs in cron-scripts.

issue_title: Rollback after user modifications
issue_body:
Suppose a user runs LE and it modifies the file `/etc/apache2/sites-available/example.conf``. The user then also modifies this file and at some point in the future the user tries to rollback back to the state before running LE for the first time. In this case, the users changes are lost.

 Perhaps we could something with comparing the values for mtime, but this is tricky as changes are registered with the `reverter` before they occur. There's not a lot we can do here, but I expect this behavior will be frustrating for some users. I'm just creating this issue for tracking purposes.

issue_title: Renewal Method?
issue_body:
Your ACME spec in the issuance section explains that a renewed certificate can be obtained by performing an unauthenticated GET request at the URI created when the cert is generated, but I can't find any evidence of this in this repo or others. Is the spec correct?

Having to use user key for a simple renewal weakens the security model, and a full authenticated renewal is also not very convenient for websites behind load balancers or with unique setups.

Clarification on what the actual renewal situation is or will be during the public beta or launch would be helpful.

Thank you.

Separate the vector constructor and Vector type in parser
issue_body:
Most of the TL definitions use the `Vector t` type, but some of them (only one at the moment), use a `vector` constructor.

__Example:__
```cpp
// vector consructor:
future_salts#ae500895 req_msg_id:long now:int salts:vector<future_salt> = FutureSalts;

// Vector type:
messageActionChatCreate#a6638b9a title:string users:Vector<int> = MessageAction;
```
__Problem:__
Currently all vector arguments are being serialized as `Vector` (with uppercase V), so it is impossible to check the CRC32 of the string:

```php
hash('crc32b', 'future_salts req_msg_id:long now:int salts:Vector future_salt = FutureSalts')
=> "94e2d547"

hash('crc32b', 'future_salts req_msg_id:long now:int salts:vector future_salt = FutureSalts')
=> "ae500895" # Passes!
```
It's not a crucial issue, but anyway :no_mouth:

if 'dbus' in globals() or not sys.platform.startswith('darwin'):

if 'dbus' in globals() and not sys.platform.startswith('darwin'):

issue_title: Method hooks swallow AttributeError
issue_body:
If `AttributeError` is raised by a `Method`/`Function` hook, it is silently swallowed:

```
class C(Schema):
    f = fields.Method('_bad_serialize')
    def _bad_serialize(self, obj):
        return self._do_something_else(obj)  # raises AttributeError

test_type = namedtuple('test_type', 'f')
C(strict=True).dump(test_type(123))  # returns empty list
```

This makes finding the bugs harder. I understand the rationale and I guess fixing it might make some code backward incompatible, but is there a way to add at least some warning mode?

issue_title: open() returns before port is ready
issue_body:
When opening a pyserial port and immediately reading from it or flushing it or whatever, the immediate action fails silently.  To reproduce (note mine is Windows 10, python 3.5, but I've seen reports of the same behavior on Linux):

```
import unittest
import serial
import time

"""
1) create a virtual or real connection between COM12 and COM13
2) in a terminal connected to COM12 (at 9600, N81), enter some junk text (e.g.'sdgfdsgasdg')
3) then execute this unit test
"""

class Test_test1(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_A(self):
        with serial.Serial(port='COM13', baudrate=9600) as s:   # open serial port
            print("Read ASAP:  {}".format(s.read(s.in_waiting)))
            time.sleep(0.1)     # wait for 100 ms for pyserial port to actually be ready
            print( "Read after delay:  {}".format(s.read(s.in_waiting)) )

if __name__ == '__main__':
    unittest.main()
```

Output will be:
```
Read ASAP:  b''
Read after delay:  b'sdgfdsgasdg'
[...]
Ran 1 test in 0.101s
```

Desired Output:
```
Read ASAP:  b'sdgfdsgasdg'
Read after delay:  b''.
[...]
Ran 1 test in 0.101s
```

Discussed this with several of your engineers last week at WWDC, who asked me to post this here.

General problem domain: Trying to detect lego pieces within an image frame. This would be used in AR to be able to pinpoint where a particular piece is out of an assortment of parts that are spread out. Initially, I am trying to get just a single piece working, though ultimately a couple of hundred different pieces are a more real world scenario.

The model I've trained for a single part exhibits two peculiar characteristics that the engineers I spoke with wanted to explore:

* When the model trained, it showed negative loss numbers for much of the training session.
* With the trained model, the IMG_1081.JPG test image in the archive is able to be accurately detected but exhibits a confidence value of just 0.2%, while an evaluate() call against the trained data set shows average precisions of 1.0.

An archive with an sframe, model and test images is [available here](https://www.dropbox.com/s/l1edyncw0bqntts/LegoObjectDetection.zip?dl=0)

issue_title: Unset value on ToOneField makes it None during hydration
issue_body:
If there's a foreign key on a resource, and the bundle data doesn't include that field, the foreign key field gets set to None during the hydration process, even if it already has a value.

The following test case illustrates this (and also how it behaves differently from a non-relational field)

``` python
def test_full_hydrate__does_not_overwrite_related_value_if_not_put(self):
        class RelatedBasicResource(BasicResource):
            parent = fields.ToOneField(BasicResource, 'parent', null=True, blank=True)
        basic = RelatedBasicResource()
        basic_bundle_1 = Bundle(data={
            'name': 'Daniel',
            'date_joined': None,
        })
        basic_bundle_1.obj = Mock()
        basic_bundle_1.obj.date_joined = aware_datetime(2010, 2, 15, 12, 0, 0)
        basic_bundle_1.obj.view_count = 3
        basic_bundle_1.obj.parent = Mock()

        self.assertNotEqual(basic_bundle_1.obj.view_count, None)
        self.assertNotEqual(basic_bundle_1.obj.parent, None)

        # Now load up the data.
        hydrated = basic.full_hydrate(basic_bundle_1)

        self.assertNotEqual(hydrated.obj.view_count, None)
        self.assertNotEqual(hydrated.obj.parent, None)
```

I believe https://github.com/django-tastypie/django-tastypie/commit/73425c31ce732646c6b2299feba4b913d9e93387 is the cause of this. I see the problem in 0.13.3 and the latest repo version but not in 0.13.2.

The field is both blank and null but it now hits the null case before the blank case and therefore gets set to None rather than falling through. I don't know tastypie well enough to have the right solution but hopefully this information can shed some light for somebody :)

issue_title: Deal with CI flake8 errors: 'do not use bare except'
issue_body:
Travis CI is using the newest flake8 version, that is 3.5.0. This version stared checking for bare exceptions (i.e. catch-all exceptions). See this PR https://github.com/django-tastypie/django-tastypie/pull/1539. The following errors were reported byt flake8 CI step:

```

./tests/core/tests/resources.py:4550:9: E722 do not use bare except'
```

To make Travis happy and to make the code better this needs to be dealt with.

issue_title: Python ChannelNormalize have different parameter order with scala ChannelNormalize
issue_body:
Python ChannelNormalize initializer:
def __init__(self, mean_r, mean_b, mean_g, std_r=1.0, std_g=1.0, std_b=1.0, bigdl_type="float"):
        super(ChannelNormalize, self).__init__(bigdl_type, mean_r, mean_g, mean_b, std_r, std_g, std_b)
The order is: mean_r, mean_b, mean_g, std_r, std_g, std_b
Scala ChannelNormalize initializer:
def apply(meanR: Float, meanG: Float, meanB: Float,
    stdR: Float = 1, stdG: Float = 1, stdB: Float = 1)
The order is: meanR, meanG, meanB, stdR, stdG, stdB

issue_title: Unify the input to be batch mode
issue_body:
Currently the input dimension is a little bit confuse in BigDL. What the meaning of the first dimension? sometimes it's for batch, sometimes not. 

i.e

```
class Squeeze(Layer):

    
    Delete singleton all dimensions or a specific dim.


    :param dim: Optional. The dimension to be delete. Default: delete all dimensions.
    :param num_input_dims: Optional. If in a batch model, set to the inputDims.

```

By default what's the meaning of the first dimentions? batch? or without batch?  maybe we should say explicitly in the document that the first dimension is batch, or we should give a more strong assumption that the first dimension is always batch.




```
class View(Layer):

    
    This module creates a new view of the input tensor using the sizes passed to the constructor.
    The method setNumInputDims() allows to specify the expected number of dimensions of the
    inputs of the modules. This makes it possible to use minibatch inputs when using a size -1
    for one of the dimensions.


    :param size: sizes use for creates a new view


    >>> view = View([1024,2])
    creating: createView
    '''

    def __init__(self,
                 sizes,
                 num_input_dims=0,
                 bigdl_type="float"):

```
For the layer View, how to control the input is in batch mode or not? if we look into the code we can see that it's set by `num_input_dims`, but maybe we should explain this more clearly.

issue_title: Add metadata to experiments
issue_body: In a project we developed a small tool to display certain types of artifacts such as pngs, mpeg movies or CSVs directly in jupyter notebooks, see https://github.com/neuroinfo-os/deep-bottleneck/blob/master/iclr_wrap_up/artifact_viewer_demo.ipynb. In the background we have mapping from artifact names to types of renderers. If metadata could be added to artifact, then one could build a generic artifact viewer, that maps from e.g. conten types to renderers. We found the combination of combining stored plots in notebooks very useful for experiment evaluation. The feature was easy to implement and is in #337. If you like the idea, then I would be happy to contribute the artifact_viewer as well.

issue_title: __module__ of classes imported from __init__.py is confusing for sphinx
issue_body:
Sphinx (using [intersphinx](http://www.sphinx-doc.org/en/stable/ext/intersphinx.html)) can't find some classes from your objects.inv, resulting in 

```text
WARNING: py:class reference target not found: dependency_injector.containers.declarative.DeclarativeContainer
```

because it is referenced as `dependency_injector.containers.DeclarativeContainer` and not `dependency_injector.containers.declarative.DeclarativeContainer` in the [objects.inv](http://python-dependency-injector.ets-labs.org/objects.inv)

This can be worked around by assigning the `__module__` property of classes imported in any `__init__.py`.  See also http://stackoverflow.com/a/24787344

issue_title: Fix Elastigroup healthCheckType
issue_body:
The current implementation always set the Elastigroup's healthCheckType to "ELB" if there are any load balancers, regardless of their types.

The [API clearly states](https://api.spotinst.com/elastigroup/amazon-web-services-2/create/#compute.launchSpecification.healthCheckType) that ELB is for classic ELBs and TARGET_GROUP should be used for ALBs. See [Spotinst's recommendation](https://github.com/zalando-stups/senza/pull/516#pullrequestreview-136726224).

issue_title: Playlist items are hidden if track lookup fails
issue_body:
When browsing playlists containing URI's to tracks that are no longer available in the library, MMW will fail silently and not display any of the available information for that track.

The failure occurs in https://github.com/pimusicbox/mopidy-musicbox-webclient/blob/01ae68ebc6834c23d407b6bed49b55f2b86fc382/mopidy_musicbox_webclient/static/js/process_ws.js#L189-L193.

The desired behavior should probably be to log the error to the console and fall back to using the track Ref instead. Since the track also will not be playable, it might make sense to disable the context menu for the affected tracks as well, and maybe add '[Not playable]' to the track name?

issue_title: Duality: "bad_ineq_inference" when using Reals

```
(set-option :fixedpoint.engine "duality")

(declare-rel Goal ())
(declare-rel A (Real Real Real))
(declare-rel B (Real Real Real))

(declare-var x Real)
(declare-var y Real)

(rule (=> (or (> y x)
              (= y z))
          (A x y z)))

(rule (=> (A x y z)
          (B x y z)))

(rule (=> (and (B x y z)
               (not (= y z))
               (= y x)
               (> y 0)
               )
          Goal))

(query Goal)
```

issue_title: Change default nonlinearity to identity
issue_body:
In #133 three people spoke out in favour of changing the default nonlinearity for layers to the identity. I'm making this a separate issue so we can stay on topic there. What does everyone else think?

If we make this change we should probably raise a warning if no nonlinearity is specified, since it will break almost everyone's code.

As @f0k pointed out it's also valuable to add a layer that does nothing more than apply a nonlinearity, I think that's a good idea as well (regardless of whether we decide to change the default).

issue_title: Smarter picking of kernel to start
issue_body:
Currently, nbval always starts the default Python kernel, though this is not necessarily running in the same Python environment as nbval itself (see discussion on #6).

1. In the default case, we should probably pick the kernel to start based on notebook metadata, like nbconvert does for `--execute`. This would allow validating notebooks in other languages.
2. We may also want an option which forces it to start a Python kernel in the same Python environment as nbconvert itself, so we know that test dependencies affect what's available in the kernel. Thoughts on what this should be called?

issue_title: Replace jinja2 templates by project templates
issue_body:
Relates to #149, as pgen will get project parsing support (a user provides own project aka template), most of jinja2 templates should be replaced by exporter dictionaries.

Each exporter will have a dictinary which is 1:1 mapping to the project files (pgen export with this dictionary should create a valid project even if no yaml files are provided). This dictionary will be adjusted to the user's input (template, yaml files), then exported.

This should eliminate jinja2 templates for iar, uvision and coide. This should simplify adding new exporters and unify them a bit, as now misc options are not 1:1 mapping to the tools projects format.

I'll provide more details once I make it working for IAR. This changes might affect targets definitions, will see how far we can get.
