issue_title: Add ways to manipulate strings without square-bracket indexing
issue_body:
In Data8 we're trying to avoid square-bracket indexing because it confuses some students.  This makes working with strings a little challenging.  Technically, a lot can be done with the workflow `string -> list -> table -> manipulate -> list -> string`, but that's pretty ugly.  I'd like to add a few functions to `datascience` to work around this.

Concretely, here are two things I'd like to be able to do.  (Ani says some connector instructors are interested in the first one, too.)
1. Index a subrange of a string.  This could be done like `Table.take` (e.g. `item_range("foobar", np.arange(1, 5))`) or like range indexing (e.g. `item_range("foobar", 1, 5)`).  This would have the added benefit of working on NumPy arrays.
2. Have a version of the `split` method that returns an array instead of a list.  Otherwise we have to wrap calls to `split` in `np.array`, which is confusing when students have only a vague idea about the difference between lists and arrays.  Since we can't teach regular expressions, `split` is a fairly core text manipulation tool.

I realize this is not really in-scope for the `datascience` library.  Other options include avoiding text manipulation; making another library for Data8-specific helper functions; or teaching lists and square-bracket indexing.  I don't really like any of those, but I'm open to other suggestions.

issue_title: support for ELF relocatable objects (i.e. Linux kernel modules)
issue_body:
As you can see in https://github.com/angr/cle/blob/82e33f92e18d7b5a8cfb9ed43663d50a52a4ab43/cle/backends/elf.py#L491, the ELF backend uses sh_addr for loading objects into the memory.

issue_title: crash in CFGFast if a block in knowledge.Function references invalid address
issue_body:
The following crash might occur during project.analyses.CFGFast():

```
  File "angr_qa.py", line 102, in <module>
    main()
  File "angr_qa.py", line 40, in main
    p.analyses.CFGFast()
  File "/home/user/work/angr-dev/angr/angr/analysis.py", line 99, in make_analysis
    oself.__init__(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/home/user/work/angr-dev/angr/angr/analyses/cfg_fast.py", line 595, in __init__
    self._analyze()
  File "/home/user/work/angr-dev/angr/angr/analyses/forward_analysis.py", line 197, in _analyze
    self._post_analysis()
  File "/home/user/work/angr-dev/angr/angr/analyses/cfg_fast.py", line 980, in _post_analysis
    self.make_functions()
  File "/home/user/work/angr-dev/angr/angr/analyses/cfg_base.py", line 835, in make_functions
    blockaddr_to_function
  File "/home/user/work/angr-dev/angr/angr/analyses/cfg_base.py", line 924, in _process_irrational_functions
    for block in function.blocks:
  File "/home/user/work/angr-dev/angr/angr/knowledge/function.py", line 106, in blocks
    yield self._get_block(block.addr)
  File "/home/user/work/angr-dev/angr/angr/knowledge/function.py", line 115, in _get_block
    block = self._project.factory.block(addr, max_size=self._block_sizes[addr])
  File "/home/user/work/angr-dev/angr/angr/lifter.py", line 96, in lift
    raise AngrMemoryError("No bytes in memory for block starting at 0x%x." % addr)
```

The function Function.blocks calls Function._get_block (https://github.com/angr/angr/blob/c60388cddc93ec2c11a28bd0ff95b4cd5ba979f2/angr/knowledge/function.py#L103), which in turns calls self._project.factory.block.
The call to self._project.factory.block might throw AngrMemoryError, however Function.blocks only catches AngrTranslationError. The unhandled AngrMemoryError causes the analysis to terminate.

I can push the easy fix to this issue, which is catching AngrMemoryError as well. 
Before I start to do this I'd like to know whether there is a deeper bug, e.g. is it expected that a Function contains blocks which are outside of the loaded memory?

issue_title: DjangoUnicodeDecodeError when model title contains non-ascii characters
issue_body:
Our models.py contains

```python
class Entry(models.Model):
    class Meta:
        verbose_name_plural = 'Einträge'
```

This causes django-admin-tools 0.7.0 to explode with

```
DjangoUnicodeDecodeError at /admin/
'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xc3 in position 5: ordinal not in range(128). 
You passed in <django.utils.functional.__proxy__ object at 0x106a53310> (<class 'django.utils.functional.__proxy__'>)
```

The cause are the `{% trans item.title|capfirst %}` in *admin_tools/menu/templates/admin_tools/menu/item.html* and *admin_tools/dashboard/templates/admin_tools/dashboard/modules/app_list.html*.

For one, I'm not sure that doing a capfirst and then translating ist the right thing in the first place: the translations should be coming from the app in question and modifying the lookup strings first with `capfirst` will break any intentional translation.

But the real problem is the actual translation. Trying to translate those model titles looks wrong, since if I wanted to translate, the right place to do that is in the model's Meta `verbose_name`/`verbose_name_plural`. Throwing a munged model's name into the translating engine and hoping for the best will probably not work right most of the time.

The attached patch removes the `{% trans %}` blocks in favour of the original strings and allows things to continue.

[no_translation_patch.txt](https://github.com/django-admin-tools/django-admin-tools/files/35445/no_translation_patch.txt)

issue_title: refactor the current tests
issue_body:
Hi all,

I'm all for the integration tests they're useful and powerful but can we consider dropping the parallel test at least to a nightly build slot yet? (this at least saves us about ~5min on the tests).

Waiting 20min just to get in 4 lines of code seems a bit expensive especially when we have multiple very small PR waiting.

Does anyone have any opinion on this?

Could we tie what tests run against the way a PR has been tagged for instance? I find it's not overly needed to run all of the XML tests for instance against a PR which just (for arguments sake) changes some debug logging lines of code in a plugin.

Thanks,

Rob

issue_title: Adding timestamps to tests
issue_body:
Is it possible to add some sort of timestamps to the test system?

I'm curious about keeping track of where (if at all) we're seeing any performance improvements/drops.

aka if the integration tests took 30sec before 1 PR but then take >5min after another I can't easily determine if this is due to one tests slowing down or due to all tests running slower.

My main reason for asking is that in order to debug this I would then need to re-run the tests at least twice on another machine as the Jenkins box likely has different specs to the machines I do dev work on.

Does this sound like a reasonable extension or are there reasons we can't try to add this?

issue_title: Inconsistency with 404 response on empty collections
issue_body:
* Set `read_only` to true
* Give readonly access to the postgresql user
* Give `read` permission to everyone on a bucket `foo`
* Going to `/buckets/foo/collections/unknown` gives 404
* Going to `/buckets/foo/collections/unknown/records` gives 503

Listing the records of an unknown collection should definitely give 404, except with the `default` bucket plugin.

Very related to https://github.com/Kinto/kinto/issues/558

issue_title: django.gettext is not a function with many-to-many autocomplete in translate.js
match Participant reports incorrect summoner when run more than once
issue_body:
If a match is looked up from a summoner's match history, and participant data is retrieved, on a subsequent run with /any/ summoner from that match, the first participant returned will be some other person in the match, but it will look an awful lot like the summoner being looked up played that champion and had those stats. 

Additionally, if you iterate through all participants, on the first run, the "some other person"'s stats will never appear, and the person being looked up will appear twice. On subsequent runs, all stats will appear, but the "some other person"'s stats will be attributed to whoever is being looked up.

Disabling ghost loading will cause this to happen immediately, instead of on subsequent runs. On the latest revision, it is necessary to disable ghost loading, because ghost loading prevents accessing summoner.name for a participant. 

Test case on latest revision from Github:

```
import cassiopeia as riot

def simpleNickToChampMap(summoner):
    summoner = riot.get_summoner(name=summoner)
    history = summoner.match_history

    lastMatchID = int(history[0].id)
    for i in riot.Match(id=lastMatchID).participants:
        print(i.summoner.name, "playing", i.champion.name)

 

print("Running once...")        
simpleNickToChampMap("return3")
print("\n"*5)
print("Running again...")
simpleNickToChampMap("return3")
print("Whoever you looked up will always appear to be playing the first champion enumerated, instead of whoever is actually playing that champion.")
print("Additionally, that champion never appears in the first enumeration. It's clobbered by whoever you looked up.")
print("Now we will look up anybody else in that match. They will also be playing Veigar.")
print("\n"*5)
simpleNickToChampMap("ZAMURIJACK")
```

Example output without ghost loading (problem will occur immediately). In the first two runs, you can see me playing two champions at once. In the third run, you can see ZAMURIJACK playing two champions at once.

| Build System |  Makefile | 

I am trying to get my new R200 camera to work on my Dell XPS L701X laptop with Ubuntu 16.04 and eventually ROS.
When I run the bin\cpp-capture utility, there is only an RGB image.  The IR images and the depth image are blank.  Usually there is a console warning "rs.warn : Subdevice 0 bad magic number 0x0" but in issue 49 it was stated this isn't enough to warrant an error.  

I am a software developer so I checked that the patch in the scipts/patch-uvcvideo-16.04.simple.sh step worked correctly.  I went through each change to the kernel files and verified that they were there.  I also verified that the new kernel was built and copied to its destination location.

I tried my camera last night on Windows 10 using the Intel Realsense SDK.  It worked fine with the same cable and using the same USB3 port on my laptop.

I found the Hardware Compatibility section in the Readme. 
https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense#hardware-requirements
My USB3 controller is NEC uPD720200 USB 3.0 Host Controller and
PCI Bridges are all Intel 5 Series/3400 Series Chipset PCI Express

I can't tell if I meet the hardware requirements or not or if not, if that is fatal.  

Any help is appreciated.  If not the hardware requirements then what else can I check?

| Platform                                 | PC |
| SDK Version                            | 2                         | 
| Language                            |  unity                          | 
| Segment			|  others                          | 

### Issue Description
Following the instructions to generate the librealsense2.sln Visual Studio project (using version 2017 community) only produced projects for 32bit. Trying to open/run the unity samples after a successful compilation doesn't work because it expects the plugins to have been built in x64 not Win32. Adding an additional option of -DCMAKE_GENERATOR_PLATFORM=x64 to the instructions for cmake fixed the issue though.

I am trying to get 3d point of objects using aligned color and depth frames that i have in order to measure those objects (length, width, etc...)

I am using  rs.rs2_deproject_pixel_to_point in order to get 3d point of from depth image, but i am not quite sure what is the meaning of the values that returned and how to connect them to the real value of depth which corresponds to the pixel.
 
I am using the following code in order to construct a 3d point of pixel from depth frame

`    depth_sensor = profile.get_device().first_depth_sensor()`
 `depth_scale = depth_sensor.get_depth_scale()`
    `depth_frame = frames.get_depth_frame()`
    `color_frame = frames.get_color_frame()`
    `depth_intrin = depth_frame.profile.as_video_stream_profile().intrinsics`
    `color_intrin = color_frame.profile.as_video_stream_profile().intrinsics`
    `depth_to_color_extrin = depth_frame.profile.get_extrinsics_to(color_frame.profile)`
    `depth_pixel = [100, 100]`
    `depth_point = rs.rs2_deproject_pixel_to_point(depth_intrin, depth_pixel, depth_scale)`

and the result is: [-5.814936230308376e-05, -3.9755548641551286e-05, 0.00012498664727900177]

what is the meaning of those values, and how can i use them on specific frames i saved on my disk? my depth frame is already aligned to the color frame (depth frame`s pixel is mapped to the same pixel in the rgb frame).
my goal is to find 3d points of two pixels in an image and calculate the real distance between them in order to measure length of object.

thank you

issue_title: Race condition in creation of Access Token with external OAuth2 server
issue_body:
In [oauth2_validators._get_token_from_authentication_server](https://github.com/jazzband/django-oauth-toolkit/blob/master/oauth2_provider/oauth2_validators.py#L335-L350) there's a race condition when two requests both present a new Bearer Access Token causing a SQL insertion error for a uniqueness constraint violation. 

This has been exercised in the wild by our project that has a client that makes near simultaneous requests of the same Django endpoint with the same Bearer token (see [validate_bearer_token](https://github.com/jazzband/django-oauth-toolkit/blob/master/oauth2_provider/oauth2_validators.py#L384-L402). 

I believe the fix is to use `AccessToken.objects.get_or_create()`. Does this make sense @jleclanche?

issue_title: Fail on "Since: UNRELEASED" if this is a release
issue_body:
In some projects we have a convention of documenting new symbols as "Since: UNRELEASED", and fixing all those to "Since: 1.2.3" during release. This avoids this fairly common failure mode:

* a developer writes a new feature and aims to land it in 1.2.3
* they write "Since: 1.2.3" in the documentation
* fixing it or reviewing it or whatever takes a bit too long
* it actually lands in 1.3.5
* the documentation still claims it was available since 1.2.3

If Hotdoc's Automake integration can somehow determine that this version is in fact a release, then it could fail the build (or at least fail `make check`) when such symbols appear.

The hard part is that in versions that are **not** a release - for instance the topic branch developing the feature, or the state of master just after it has been merged - these symbols should be allowed.

This is a check for developers rather than for distributions, so we probably don't need to go to the same lengths as `AX_IS_RELEASE`. For instance I think it would be fine to only do this check if `$(top_srcdir)/.git` exists and `git describe --long` succeeds and the result of `git describe --long` matches regexp `-0-g[0-9a-f]*$` (i.e. our current position is a tag).

issue_title: OpenThread API Naming Cleanup
issue_body:
I'd like to clean up the OpenThread API naming.

Some issue I see with what exists today:
- Not grouped by component (although, some examples of grouping by component does exist, such as `otCoap*`).
- Some boolean properties are get/set while others are enable/disable.
- Implementation is in one large file `src/openthread.cpp`.
- API header files use `include/openthread-` prefix, while platform API header filenames are `include/platform/` prefix.

Proposed convention:

- API Naming Convention
  - Commands
    - ot[Component][Command] (e.g. `otMacEnergyScan(...)`)
  - Properties
    - ot[Component]Get[Property] (e.g. `otMacGetChannel(...)`)
    - ot[Component]Set[Property] (e.g. `otMacSetChannel(...)`)

- File Naming Convention
  - Client API header files
    - include/ot-api-[component].h (e.g. `include/ot-api-mac.h`)
  - Platform API header files
    - include/ot-plat-[component].h (e.g. `include/ot-plat-radio.h`)
  - API implementation files
    - src/api/[component].cpp: (e.g. `src/api/mac.cpp`)

Thoughts and feedback appreciated.

issue_title: [nrf52480] usb-cdc-uart: Sending/receiving beacons locks up NCP
issue_body:
It would appear that when used in usb-cdc-uart mode that receiving or sending a beacon locks up the NCP hard (as opposed to #2380 where only usb-cdc-uart transmit was jammed).

Responding to a beacon request:

```
[0000068246] [INFO]-MAC-----: Received Beacon Request
[0000068246] [DEBG]-MAC-----: Request to start operation "TransmitBeacon"
[0000068247] otPlatRadioReceive
[0000068247] [DEBG]-MAC-----: Starting operation "TransmitBeacon"
[0000068249] [INFO]-MAC-----: Sending Beacon, name:LoWPAN_352, xpanid:87ecc1eb76b5d7c0, id:3 ver:2, joinable:no, ? 
***LOCKED UP***
```

Performing a scan where we received a beacon response:

```
[0000039579] [DEBG]-MAC-----: Request to start operation "ActiveScan"
[0000039579] otPlatRadioReceive
[0000039579] [DEBG]-MAC-----: Starting operation "ActiveScan"
[0000039579] otPlatUartSend: 8 bytes
[0000039580] APP_USBD_CDC_ACM_USER_EVT_TX_DONE
[0000039580] otPlatUartSendDone

It is unclear to me if this is beacon-specific, or if this represents a general problem with sending/receiving packets.

Note that this behavior only seems to occur when using `USB=1`. When using the plain vanilla UART I don't see these issues.

issue_title: How can i Save TFLEARN model . and reload it in Production Enviroment .
Confusion Matrix for HDF5 Convolutional Neural Network
issue_body:
I am currently working with TFLearn to test different CNNs on a dataset. I have converted the dataset to HDF5 and managed to train a few practice networks. My question is how do I predict and plot a confusion matrix? The code I have so far is as follows:

```
from __future__ import division, print_function, absolute_import

import tflearn
from tflearn.layers.core import input_data, dropout, fully_connected
from tflearn.layers.conv import conv_2d, max_pool_2d
from tflearn.layers.normalization import local_response_normalization
from tflearn.layers.estimator import regression

import h5py
import numpy
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix

# Import test data
hdf5Test = h5py.File('/home/fletch67/dfhProject/Data/twoClasses/twoTest.h5',
                      'r')

# Split into features and labels
X = hdf5Test['X']
Y = hdf5Test['Y']

# Building convolutional network
network = input_data(shape=[None, 240, 320, 3], name='input')
network = conv_2d(network, 32, 3, activation='relu', regularizer="L2")
network = max_pool_2d(network, 2)
network = local_response_normalization(network)
network = conv_2d(network, 64, 3, activation='relu', regularizer="L2")
network = max_pool_2d(network, 2)
network = local_response_normalization(network)
network = fully_connected(network, 128, activation='tanh')
network = dropout(network, 0.8)
network = fully_connected(network, 256, activation='tanh')
network = dropout(network, 0.8)
network = fully_connected(network, 2, activation='softmax')
network = regression(
  network,
  optimizer='sgd',
  learning_rate=0.01,
  loss='categorical_crossentropy',
  name='target'
)

# Training
model = tflearn.DNN(network, tensorboard_verbose=0)
model.load('/home/fletch67/dfhProject/Models/twoClasses/basicCNN/basicCNN_test'
           '.tflearn')

# Predict Values
predictions = model.predict(X)
print(predictions)
```

When ever I try to print predictions I get the following error:

```
terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::bad_alloc'
  what():  std::bad_alloc
Aborted (core dumped)
```

I've read that sklearn can be used to produce a confusion matrix, however, have been unable to find direct instructions, any help or guidance would be appreciated.

issue_title: Support WSS
issue_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When serving visdom over HTTPS (such as with a reverse proxy), the websocket connection between the client and server continues to be insecure since the `ws` instead of `wss` protocol is used. Chrome will warn you and require you to opt-in to enable the insecure websocket connection.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
A command-line option to the visdom server that causes the served javascript to replace `ws://` with `wss://`. The Python client could use `wss` instead of `ws` if it sees that the Visdom host starts with `https` instead of `http`.

issue_title: Postmark: Reply-To must be moved out of email headers
issue_body:
Postmark's API disallows Reply-To in the message headers; it's only permitted in Postmark's ReplyTo param.

``` python
# This works:
EmailMessage(..., reply_to=['reply@example.com']).send()

# This doesn't:
EmailMessage(..., headers={'Reply-To': 'reply@example.com'}).send()
```

Using the headers was the only option before Django added the explicit `reply_to` param. There may be some old code out there still using the headers approach.

Anymail should relocate Reply-To from the generic headers, if needed.

issue_title: Richer interface for array_t
array_t assignment operators
issue_body:
Hi!
I'm in process of writing type converter for my own array class based on @jagerman approach to bind Eigen types. Let's consider avoid-copy `type_caster::load` function for `Eigen::Ref< MatrixType>` which does in general the following things:
1) construct temp array `aref` from source handle;
2) saves `aref` into member variable copy_or_ref by using either
`copy_or_ref = std::move(aref)` for no-copy case or
`copy_or_ref = aref` if copy of source array is required;
3) construct Eigen target type from `copy_or_ref` and return it.

After I copy-pasted above implementation to my own type caster I discovered that step 2 break things. I pass simple numpy vector of size 10 into my test function. And no matter do we need copy or not after assignment:
1) `copy_or_ref` always has size = 1;
2) `copy_or_ref.data()` differs from `aref.data()`;
3) first element of `copy_or_ref` is a copy of first element from `aref`.
It seems like assignment creates new array of size = 1 and copies the first element from source array.

If I eliminate assignment operators from my type caster and create `array_t` copies using copy constructor only, then everything works as expected!

What `array_t` assignment operator supposed to do? Should it make different things compared to copy constructor?

issue_body:

```
error C2884: 'pybind11::detail::_': introduced by using-declaration conflicts with local function 'pybind11::detail::_'
```
The solution is to set Conformance Mode to "No".

For whatever reason this flag was enabled when I created a new project.

issue_title: ./letsencrypt-auto --help should not require root
issue_body:
This overlaps with item #1286 but comes at things from a different angle.

According to https://letsencrypt.github.io/acme-spec/, the raison d'être for ACME is to provide a good user interface, to provide good usability.

From a UI point of view, it is not reasonable to require root privileges for ./letsencrypt-auto --help

It is irrelevant and pointless to say that because of the way it's written, it requires root privileges.  The relevant point is, it shouldn't be written that way.  A good UI should be written in such a way that it can provide an informative message no matter what.  One way (not the only way) of doing this would be to incorporate a helpful message within the letsencrypt-auto shell script ... and to check for the --help option before checking for root privileges.

An issue I encountered today: the rewrite rules added to apache config by certbot-auto to coerce http requests to https doesn't seem to be bulletproof.

In particular, GET params that are url-encoded, eg ?email=testest%2Byhbugfixer%40gmail.com, seem to end up getting encoded twice so that my application receives 'testest%2Byhbugfixer%40gmail.com' for the param value instead of the intended 'testest+yhbugfixer@gmail.com', if I make the initial request in http://

Notably, this does not happen if in place of the rewrite rules added by certbot-auto, I simply create a new virtual host like so:

 <VirtualHost *:80>
        ServerName mydomain.com
        Redirect / https://mydomain.com/
 </VirtualHost>

issue_title: Properly handling user set LE_PYTHONs for RHEL/CentOS 6
issue_body:
LE_PYTHON is always set during phase 2 because this variable is exported. Even worse, at this point we can't differentiate between the case where this value was set by the user and one where it was set by a previous version of the script. This is a problem because we won't know which Python executable to use to run Certbot and whether an unsupported value was manually set by the user or not. As the script is right now, this can cause us to create the venv using Python 2.6 or continue to use Python 2.6 to run fetch.py.

A couple potential fixes are:

Fix 1:
* Before we ever set --cb-auto-has-root or --le-auto-phase2, if LE_PYTHON is set, complain about deprecation and tell people to use a new var like CB_PYTHON.
* When bootstrapping for Python 3, overwrite/unset LE_PYTHON.

Fix 2:
* Quietly overwrite/unset LE_PYTHON

The properties I would like are:
 * If in the future, when someone manually sets LE_PYTHON to Python 2.6, we tell them their python is too old like we do for other versions.
* If we can't tell whether the value was user set or not, we ignore the value. There is a tradeoff to be made between printing a warning about this and just doing it quietly. By printing a warning, how many users will get a warning/cron email when most of them haven't set LE_PYTHON as it isn't formally documented anyway.

Could you please consider to

- b, to use [Enter] but have user confirm the action ? 

Thanks !

issue_title: Payment metadata
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

The current API use identifiers to correlate a payment with some service or product. For this to work the nodes (initiator and target) need to use an out-of-band communication channel to agree on the identifier, otherwise the target won't know what the payment is for, this results in a hard to use api.

## Solution

Allow the initiator to provide metadata for a given payment, so that applications don't need to use an out-of-band communication and rely on identifiers. 

## Tasklist

Accept metada data in the transfer API.
Save the metadata as part of the state change that starts the transfer.
Extend the protocol messages to send the metada to the target node.
Extend the events API to expose this metadata.

issue_title: Reuse previously opened RPC connection for new RPC calls
issue_body:
When running multiple raiden nodes on a single Linux machine, somewhere between 20 and 30 nodes, will cause exhaustion of available TCP ports. The default for Linux is to use ports between `32768 - 61000` which gives a total of around 28K ports. Every time a new RPC connection is made new TCP port is opened for that connection and stays in `TIME_WAIT` state. The default recycle time for Linux is big enough that available ports are exhausted.

For doing orchestrated runs I added two extra sysctl configuration options:

```
net.ipv4.tcp_tw_recycle = 1
net.ipv4.tcp_tw_reuse = 1
```

That is good enough for running in orchestration but a better solution should be devised. The best option would probably be to reuse the same connections that were already opened before.

issue_title: Take default values into account in DjangoModelFactory.build()
issue_body:
Let's assume I have a model with an autogenerated field:

```python
    token = models.CharField(
        verbose_name=_("token"),
        help_text=_("Auto-generated token"),
        max_length=12,
        default=crypto.get_random_string,
        editable=False,
    )
```

and I leave out this field in the definition of the corresponding factory.

If I use `DjangoModelFactory.create()` then Django sets the default value before saving the instance to the database, and everything is fine.

If I use `DjangoModelFactory.build()` (the database is hot lava, etc.) then the default value isn't set.

I can understand why factory_boy has this behavior. However I find it frustrating. My best option for making `DjangoModelFactory.build()` usable right now is to declare:

```python
    token = factory.LazyFunction(crypto.get_random_string)
```

or:

```python
    token = factory.LazyFunction(MyModel._meta.get_field('token').default)
```

The former duplicates logic — if I change the `default` value in the model, my factory will go out of sync. The latter is boilerplate-ish. 

It's especially frustrating in cases like this one where the value is autogenerated on creation and never set in any other way...

The obvious solution is `factory.FieldDefaultOnlyForBuild` but that seems too specific to the use case described here.

Is this a problem you've seen before? Are you interested in investigating solutions?

issue_title: Functions silently overwritten in Instrument
issue_body:
### Steps to reproduce

```python
from qcodes.instrument.base import Instrument
from qcodes.instrument.function import Function


class MyInst(Instrument):
    
    def __init__(self, name):
        super().__init__(name)
        
        self.add_parameter('start', get_cmd="I'm a parameter")
        self.add_function('start', call_cmd="I'm a function")
        
    def write(self, cmd):
        print(cmd)
    
    def ask(self, cmd):
        print(cmd)
        
myinst = MyInst('myinst')

myinst.start()
```

### Expected behaviour
Since both functions and parameters get delegated as attributes in the same way, it should not be possible to add a parameter with the name of an existing function of vice versa.

### Actual behaviour
The function gets ignored in favour of the parameter. I found a comment indicating that this is by design https://github.com/QCoDeS/Qcodes/blob/cfc97ec2090ed3620634affa3177419dddb6abae/qcodes/tests/test_instrument.py#L75. I don't think this is such a good design decision.

### Proposed solution

Maybe just get rid of the functions altogether? 

### System
**operating system**
Windows 10

**qcodes branch**
master

**qcodes commit**
 cfc97ec2090ed3620634affa3177419dddb6abae

issue_title: Possible bug in LanguageModelingDataset
issue_body:
In the code for [`LanguageModelingDataset`](https://github.com/pytorch/text/blob/master/torchtext/datasets/language_modeling.py), the original text seems to be pre-processed twice, viz.:

- `text += text_field.preprocess(line)` [at line 22](https://github.com/pytorch/text/blob/master/torchtext/datasets/language_modeling.py#L22)
- `examples = [data.Example.fromlist([text], fields)]` [at line 26](https://github.com/pytorch/text/blob/master/torchtext/datasets/language_modeling.py#L26), which in turn calls 
`setattr(ex, name, field.preprocess(val))` [at line 44 of example.py](https://github.com/pytorch/text/blob/master/torchtext/data/example.py#L44)

In fact, if I try to create a simple LanguageModelingDataset, I am getting an error as follows:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/home/riddasgu/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/torchtext/datasets/language_modeling.py", line 28, in __init__
    examples = [data.Example.fromlist([text], fields)]
  File "/home/riddasgu/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/torchtext/data/example.py", line 44, in fromlist
    setattr(ex, name, field.preprocess(val))
  File "/home/riddasgu/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/torchtext/data/field.py", line 91, in preprocess
    x = self.tokenize(x)
  File "/home/riddasgu/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/torchtext/data/field.py", line 63, in <lambda>
    tokenize=(lambda s: s.split()), include_lengths=False,
AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'split'
```

issue_title: [Feature] Extend transformations to handle mutli-band non-uint8 images
issue_body:
Here I would like to discuss how to extend `ToPILImage`, `ToTensor` and transformations taking pil image as input to work with mutli-band non-uint8 images. Today, to following code does not work:
```
data = np.random.randint(0, 100, size=(10, 10, 3)).astype(np.float32)
t = Compose([
        ToPILImage(),
        RandomCrop(size=(5, 5))
    ])
print(t(data))
```
The error is `TypeError: Input type float32 is not supported` from `ToPILImage`, because PIL does not have a mode multi-band image with depth `float32`.

As PIL documentation suggests ([ref](http://pillow.readthedocs.io/en/latest/handbook/concepts.html#concept-modes)):
>  if you need to handle band combinations that are not listed above, use a sequence of Image objects.

we can modify `F.to_pil_image` such that it could recognize the mode for a single band and then produce a list of pil images:
```
if mode is None:
    expected_mode = _get_expected_mode(npimg.dtype)
    if expected_mode is None:
        raise TypeError('Input type {} is not supported'.format(npimg.dtype))
    
    return [Image.fromarray(npimg[:, :, c], mode=expected_mode) for c in range(npimg.shape[2])]
```

Next we need to change every transformation that uses PIL image as input:
- either each transformation handle whether `img` is PIL image or list
- or with a factorized code: all transformations (that uses PIL image as input) inherit from
```
class TransformPIL(object):
    @staticmethod
    def handle(img, transformation, **kwargs):
        if isinstance(img, (list, tuple)):
            return [transformation(band, **kwargs) for band in img]
        else:
            transformation(img, **kwargs)
```
and then, for example, `Resize` is 
```
class Resize(TransformPIL):
    def __init__(self, size, interpolation=Image.BILINEAR):
        # ... AS BEFORE ...

    def __call__(self, img):
        """
        Args:
            img (PIL Image): Image to be scaled.

        Returns:
            PIL Image: Rescaled image.
        """        
        return self.handle(img, F.resize, size=self.size, interpolation=self.interpolation)
```

Finally, `ToTensor` class will gather list of pil images into a single tensor.

What do you think about this approach ? Thanks!

issue_title: HTML content in HTTP 500 response
issue_body:
### Actual behavior

When issuing a Create Session HMC operation against an HMC that has the Web Services API disabled in its API configuration, HTTP status code 500 is returned with HTML in the response. The Content-Type header is set to "text/html" in this case.

The zhmcclient trips over a ParseError when trying to decode this as JSON.

### Expected behavior

The zhmcclient should tolerate HTML in an error response, based on inspecting the Content-Type header.

### Execution environment

* HMC version: 2.12.1
* zhmcclient version: 0.10.1.dev18
* Operating system (type+version): RHEL 7.2

issue_title: Consider replacing ray.get_gpu_ids() with a more general ray.get_env().
issue_body: Right now, we expose the IDs of the GPUs that a task may use via `ray.get_gpu_ids()`. However, we may want to replace that with a more general `ray.get_env()` which returns a dictionary containing the key `"GPU_IDS"`. Currently, there may not be anything else in the dictionary, but it would perhaps be more extensible.

issue_title: Segfault in plasma manager when getting many objects on multiple machines.
issue_body:
The plasma manager can be made to segfault as follows.

1. Start a large number of nodes (it may actually be possible to reproduce this on one or two nodes). However, on 100 m4.xlarge instances, this happens consistently.

2. Start Ray on all of the machines. You may want to start the Ray processes on the head node separately so that you can start the plasma manager in GDB. To do this, follow the instructions in https://github.com/ray-project/ray/issues/108#issuecomment-308572084.

3. Run the following workload (after initializing Ray).

    ```python
    @ray.remote
    def f():
        return 1

    %time l = ray.get([f.remote() for _ in range(1000000)])
    ```

This will eventually (maybe after a couple minutes) cause the plasma manager on the head node to segfault. A backtrace looks like the following.

```

/home/dev/.pyxbld/temp.linux-x86_64-2.7/pyrex/ssh2/session.c:1:2: error: #error Do not use this file, it is the result of a failed Cython compilation. 
 #error Do not use this file, it is the result of a failed Cython compilation.                                                                              
  ^
```

I think this happens because the binary wheel also includes the .pyx files, instead of just the .so files. Cython picks these up and tries to compile them instead of just using the .so file. If there is no particular reason to include the .pyx files in the wheel, I would ask if they can be excluded.

I've looked to see if I can restrict what pyximport automatically compiles, but haven't found anything so far.

issue_title: Unable to serialize JSON
issue_body:
The old (l20n) parser returned JSON, which Pontoon used internally, and the serializer took it.

The new parser returns an object and the serializer takes it as input. Since we still use JSON for storing AST in Pontoon, we need a method to conver AST object to JSON (already exists) and JSON to AST object (doesn't exist yet).

issue_title: Make all tests runnable with `processes` nosetest option
issue_body:
Developers should be able to run all of the nosetests with the `processes` option to speed things up. This does not work with some of the tests, because they expect to have something setup which is done when they are run sequentially. We should identify the pieces that need to be run sequentially and require that explicitly using the `nose.with_setup` decorator, so that developers can call `nosetests --processes=N` to speed things up. For example, on my machine running

```
nosetests -v test_aalst_patterns test_data test_checkpointing --processes=8
```
instead of
```
nosetests -v test_aalst_patterns test_data test_checkpointing
```
results in a speedup from 20 sec -> 8 sec.

issue_title: Decide the distribution model
issue_body:
The package is currently being distributed per aspect, e.g. `pip install opencensus-trace`. For other languages, our policy is to distribute trace, tags and stats in the same package. There are two reasons we are considering this option:

i. Trace, tags and stats are very thin and bundling them together is not costly.
ii. OpenCensus is a unified tracing, stats and propagation library and giving user the opportunity to install a single package is aligned with its unified nature.

Hence, I am suggesting we should distribute the package as "opencensus" with trace, stats, and tags.

issue_title: Remote sampler is broken
issue_body:
## Requirement - what kind of business use case are you trying to solve?

The remote sampler does not work

## Problem - what in Jaeger blocks you from solving the requirement?

It looks like there are two sampling endpoints on `jaeger-agent`:
`/sampling` and `/`. 
The former returns the strategy as a string (e.g. `{"strategyType":"PROBABILISTIC"}`), whereas the latter returns it as an integer (e.g. `{"strategyType":0}`)

The python client appears to be expecting the integer version, but it is querying the `/sampling` endpoint. This causes the following error when refreshing the sampling strategy:
```
  File ".../jaeger_client/sampler.py", line 438, in _update_sampler
    self._update_rate_limiting_or_probabilistic_sampler(response)
  File ".../jaeger_client/sampler.py", line 470, in _update_rate_limiting_or_probabilistic_sampler
    raise ValueError('Unsupported sampling strategy type: %s' % s_type)
ValueError: Unsupported sampling strategy type: PROBABILISTIC

```

## Proposal - what do you suggest to solve the problem or improve the existing situation?

We should use string constants for comparison here: https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-python/blob/master/jaeger_client/sampler.py#L452

## Any open questions to address

<!-- Questions that should be answered before proceeding with implementation. -->

issue_title: DynamoDB::createTable with `StreamEnabled: true` hangs
issue_body: When running locally, I've noticed that if I try to create a dynamodb table _with_ streams enabled, I get a network timeout, but if the stream is disabled I get a table created right away. Interestingly, the table appears to be created — the NodeJS client is just never informed of it. Steps to Reproduce ================== Start up the container: ```bash docker run -it \ -e SERVICES="dynamodb,dynamodbstreams" \ -p 4569:4569 \ -p 4570:4570 \ -p 8080:8080 \ -e DATA_DIR="/tmp/localstack/data" \ -v ${HOME}/data/localstack:/tmp/localstack \ localstack/localstack ``` Browse to [the web shell](http://localhost:4569/shell/) for DynamoDB. Try to create a DynamoDB table with streams _disabled_: ```javascript var params = { TableName: 'test-without-executions', KeySchema: [ { AttributeName: 'id', KeyType: 'HASH' } ], AttributeDefinitions: [ { AttributeName: 'id', AttributeType: 'S' } ], ProvisionedThroughput: { ReadCapacityUnits: 10, WriteCapacityUnits: 10 }, StreamSpecification: { StreamEnabled: false } }; dynamodb.createTable(params, function(err, data) { if (err) ppJson(err); // an error occurred else ppJson(data); // successful response }); ``` and celebrate, for the table is created! No logs from docker. Next, try to create a _very similar_ table with streams _enabled_: ```javascript var params = { TableName: 'test-with-executions', KeySchema: [ { AttributeName: 'id', KeyType: 'HASH' } ], AttributeDefinitions: [ { AttributeName: 'id', AttributeType: 'S' } ], ProvisionedThroughput: { ReadCapacityUnits: 10, WriteCapacityUnits: 10 }, StreamSpecification: { StreamEnabled: true, StreamViewType: 'NEW_IMAGE' } }; dynamodb.createTable(params, function(err, data) { if (err) ppJson(err); // an error occurred else ppJson(data); // successful response }); ``` and weep - the network console hangs and eventually delivers the following error: Again, no logs from the docker side of things. The table appears to have been created successfully :confused:?

issue_title: Replace pytorch-cpu to pytorch dependency when on MacOS
issue_body:
pytorch-cpu doesn't exist for MacOS


Related to https://github.com/kipoi/models/issues/81

Implement a method `osx` here:

https://github.com/kipoi/kipoi/blob/master/kipoi/components.py#L636-L654

and check for the osx flag bellow this line:
https://github.com/kipoi/kipoi/blob/8c4a385a3cf28d913da6a5fa758a00260de07748/kipoi/cli/env.py#L108-L110

```python
from sys import platform
if platform == "darwin":
    logger.info("Using osx-type dependencies") 
    deps = deps.osx()
```

issue_title: [URGENT] OP version issue
issue_body:
In #907, we decided to align to Numpy broadcasting.  This is a break change to many operators, like PRelu, but we didn't upgrade these OP's version. 

So, once a backend upraded its code to support onnx 1.2, it will no longer be able to run the old models.  Because there is only one version, can only have one implementation.

issue_title: cwltoil fails when @ symbol is in the directory name
issue_body:
When running a CWL workflow with `toilcwl`, the job fails when the `@` symbol is in the file name. The below steps are ran on my Mac. However, we have experienced the same issue on CentOS 7.

Steps to reproduce:
```
$ virtualenv venv
$ source venv/bin/activate
$ pip install toil[all]

$ cwltoil --version
DEBUG:rdflib:RDFLib Version: 4.2.2
3.16.0

$ mkdir example
$ mkdir example@gmail.com
```

`touch.cwl`:
```
cwlVersion: v1.0
class: CommandLineTool
baseCommand: ["touch"]
inputs:
  - id: filename
    type: string
    inputBinding:
      position: 1
outputs:
  - id: file_out
    type: File
    outputBinding:
      glob: $(inputs.filename)
  - id: standard_out
    type: stdout
stdout: $(inputs.filename+".touch.cwl.stdout")
requirements:
  - class: InlineJavascriptRequirement
```

`touch-works.yml`:
```
filename: example/anewfile
```

`touch-broken.yml`:
```
filename: example@gmail.com/anewfile
```

Run:
```
$ cd example
$ cwltoil ../touch.cwl ../touch-works.yml
```

issue_title: Deal with non-linear power flow in direct current networks without work-around
issue_body:
If you want to compute the non-linear power flow in a meshed direct current network, you have to pretend it is an AC network and set all complex parts of the impedance (z and b) to zero and all load/generator reactive power set points to zero. All buses should be PQ controlled. Then the AC power flow will give the same answer as the DC power flow.
It would be better to do the Newton-Raphson with the correct non-linear DC power flow equations.
See https://www.pypsa.org/doc/power_flow.html#non-linear-power-flow-for-dc-networks and the mailing list discussion https://groups.google.com/d/msg/pypsa/L_MndTvxwBA/L8WU-YGGCwAJ.

issue_title: Build custom database
issue_body:
I'm trying to build a custom database.

Based on your documentation (https://prophyle.github.io/custom_db.html) I succeed to: 
1) Download and extract sequences
2) Index sequences with _samtools faidx_
3) Generate the _acc2taxid.tsv_ file

My problem is with the _prophyle_ncbi_tree.py_ script. Taxids are not found for every sequence (accession number). I got error message like this:

> [prophyle_ncbi_tree] ERROR: TaxID not found for sequence XXX_XXXXXXXX

Even if _XXX_XXXXXXXX.version_ is available in the _acc2taxid.tsv_ file

**First question**: is it possible that this problem is because the acc2taxid.tsv file contains _accession.version_ number instead of _accession_ number only?

**Second question**: Also, I'm not sure to understand the first 2 parameters needed by _prophyle_ncbi_tree.py_.
What's expected for <library_name> and _<library_main_dir>_ ? _<library_name>_ must be just a directory name and _<library_main_dir>_ must be the full path to this directory name?

**Last question**: is it possible to select an existing NCBI taxonomy instead of automatically download a new one in the current working directory?

issue_title: Add 'keepalive' option to SSHClientBase()
issue_body:
SSHClientBase can re-use existing connection to the node if exists, or create a new one.
But it doesn't close the opened connections if the class is used as a context manager. This allows to use many different ssh accesses to the same nodes without re-connecting for each command.

Existing approach is suitable for small amount of nodes when many different ssh command are using the same connections.
For cases when there are many different nodes, it will be necessary to close the connections after leaving the SSHClient context.

Let's add to the SSHClientBase() a new option: 'keepalive', default to True, which will leave the existing connection working if True, and close the existing connection after leaving the context manager if False.

For example:

remote = SSHClient(....)
with remote.keepalive(enforce=True):  # default
   # connection will be preserved at the context exit
   ...
with remote.keepalive(enforce=False):
   # connection will be closed at the context exit
   ...

issue_title: Python 3.2.2, virtualenv 1.6.4 - venv is missing some library symlinks
issue_body:
When testing my Django 3 port (on Ubuntu Oneiric x64) in a venv created by virtualenv 1.6.4 using a Python 3.2.2 interpreter, a lot of failures were caused by the fact that a number of needed stdlib modules did not appear in the venv's lib directory. These were:

tokenize.py
functools.py 
heapq.py 
bisect.py 
weakref.py 
reprlib.py 
token.py 

When I manually symlinked these from /usr/lib/python3.2 to the venv's lib, the spurious test failures disappeared. Some of these modules are now added for minver == 3, but I think the above list should be added to REQUIRED_MODULES when minver == 2.

#144 might be related, or it might not.

issue_title: gratuitous prefixes in n3//turtle serializers
issue_body:
bdar...@gmail.com, 2009-06-01T15:16:14.000Z 
The N3 and Turtle serializers attempt to use namespace prefixes for subject
URIs. As a consequence, one can end up with dozens, or even hundreds, of
meaningless namespace prefixes.

Suggestion: remove auto-prefixing for subject URIs on these serializers;
only include it if there's an explicit binding.

Comment 1 by gromgull
This is a good point - we should have a flag deciding whether or not to try to
generate more prefix mappings.


Comment 2 by lindstr...@gmail.com
I propose to set this to FIXED in light of the revised Turtle & N3 serializers (r1708).


Comment 3 by gromgull
Fixed as per lindstreams comment.

issue_title: Signature Signing Incorrect?
issue_body:
I was testing my Java Port of the signature signing mechanism. I was using the Python version of the signature signing as a reference test. I could not get the Java Version of the signature to match the Python version. I also tried several online tools for signature signing and they would agree with the Java Version of my output and not the Python version. After a long head scratching, i think the bug is in Python version.

The python code in question in js.py
key = hashlib.sha1(PRIVATE_KEY).digest()
signer = hmac.new(key, message, hashlib.sha1)

shouldn't it be the following?
signer = hmac.new(PRIVATE_KEY, message, hashlib.sha1)


The test case i was using is:
PRIVATE_KEY = '0cc86846024a4c95a5dfd3111a532d13'
js.sign_billing_info_update("ABC", 1312701386)

the message it would produce is :
[1312701386,billinginfoupdate,[account_code:ABC]]
Output:
'94c1af938a64b0d535dab615f7ba62cea9ffbc6f-1312701386'

So the signature is
94c1af938a64b0d535dab615f7ba62cea9ffbc6f

And when using online tool or my Java Program is the following:
6afcdb714a0eae4d68048649f25677c3fa780257

Online Tool Used: 
http://hash.online-convert.com/sha1-generator

BTW, i was trying to replicate the signature generation example on the recurly site using the test key and test timestamp.
http://docs.recurly.com/recurlyjs/signatures/spec

-Talib
